item.title: Time for Some Spring Cleaning
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Spring is the perfect time to refresh your oral health routine. Just like you declutter your home, your smile deserves a fresh start too — swap out your old toothbrush, stock up on floss, and schedule a professional cleaning.

Good habits like brushing twice daily, flossing every night, and visiting your dentist every six months form the foundation of a healthy smile. Contact Britt Moore Dental today to book your spring checkup.
item.excerpt:encoded: Spring is the perfect time to refresh your oral health routine.

item.title: Are You Nervous About Seeing the Dentist? You’re Not Alone
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental anxiety affects millions of Americans, and many people avoid necessary care because of fear. At Britt Moore Dental, we understand your concerns and offer a calm, welcoming environment designed to put you at ease.

From gentle communication to sedation dentistry options, we have tools to make your visit comfortable. Don't let anxiety stand between you and a healthy smile — call us today to discuss how we can help.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental anxiety affects millions of Americans. At Britt Moore Dental, we offer sedation options to help.

item.title: How Missing Teeth Can Affect Your Health
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Missing teeth affect more than your smile's appearance. Gaps left by missing teeth allow neighboring teeth to shift, alter your bite, and lead to bone loss in the jaw over time. Difficulty chewing can also affect your nutrition.

Dental implants, bridges, and dentures are all excellent solutions for replacing missing teeth. Contact Britt Moore Dental to explore which option is right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Missing teeth affect more than your smile's appearance — they can impact your overall health.

item.title: What Exactly Is Biofilm?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Biofilm is a thin, sticky layer of bacteria that forms on surfaces — including your teeth. Known as dental plaque, biofilm is the primary cause of tooth decay and gum disease when allowed to accumulate.

Regular brushing, flossing, and professional cleanings are essential to controlling biofilm. Ask your hygienist about the best tools and techniques to keep biofilm at bay.
item.excerpt:encoded: Biofilm — or dental plaque — is the primary cause of tooth decay and gum disease.

item.title: Bright Ideas for Your Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A bright, confident smile can make a powerful first impression. Whether you're interested in professional whitening, veneers, or simply improving your daily oral hygiene, there are many ways to enhance your smile.

At Britt Moore Dental, we offer a full range of cosmetic dentistry services tailored to your goals and budget. Schedule a consultation today to discover your smile's potential.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover bright ideas to enhance your smile with cosmetic dentistry at Britt Moore Dental.

item.title: Clean Toothbrush, Healthy Toothbrush
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Your toothbrush does the important work of removing plaque and bacteria from your teeth, but it can also harbor germs itself. Rinse your brush thoroughly after each use, store it upright to air dry, and replace it every three to four months.

Never share your toothbrush, and replace it after any illness. A clean, fresh toothbrush is one of the simplest ways to protect your oral health.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to keep your toothbrush clean and when to replace it for optimal oral health.

item.title: Three Must-Have Dental Treatments
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Some dental treatments are worth prioritizing for nearly every patient. Regular professional cleanings, fluoride treatments, and dental exams are the cornerstone of preventive care and can save you significant time and expense down the road.

Ask your dentist at Britt Moore Dental which treatments are most important for your individual needs. Preventive care today prevents costly restorative care tomorrow.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover three must-have dental treatments that form the foundation of preventive care.

item.title: Oral Health Concerns for Teens
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The teenage years bring unique oral health challenges. From orthodontic treatment to wisdom teeth development, sports injuries, and the temptation of sugary drinks, teens face many factors that can impact their smiles.

Establishing good habits during adolescence sets the stage for lifelong dental health. Schedule regular checkups for your teen at Britt Moore Dental and discuss any concerns together.
item.excerpt:encoded: The teenage years bring unique oral health challenges. Learn what to watch for.

item.title: Choose Chocolate on Valentine’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
This Valentine's Day, reach for dark chocolate instead of sticky candies. Dark chocolate is actually one of the better sweet treats for your teeth — it washes off more easily than caramel or gummies, and antioxidants in cocoa may even benefit your gum health.

Of course, moderation is key. Rinse with water after indulging and keep up with your regular brushing and flossing routine. Happy Valentine's Day from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Dark chocolate is one of the better sweet choices for your dental health this Valentine's Day.

item.title: February Is Children’s Dental Health Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
February is National Children's Dental Health Month — a great reminder to focus on your child's oral health. Early dental habits established in childhood lay the groundwork for a lifetime of healthy smiles.

Make sure your child brushes for two minutes twice a day, flosses daily, and sees the dentist every six months. Contact Britt Moore Dental to schedule your child's next checkup.
item.excerpt:encoded: February is National Children's Dental Health Month. Learn how to build healthy habits early.

item.title: What Are Dental Sealants?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental sealants are thin, protective coatings applied to the chewing surfaces of the back teeth (molars). They seal out food and bacteria that can get trapped in the deep grooves of molars, providing an effective barrier against cavities.

Sealants are especially beneficial for children and teenagers, though adults can benefit too. Ask about dental sealants at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental sealants protect back teeth from cavities by sealing out food and bacteria.

item.title: Camping Oral Health Tips
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Camping is a wonderful way to enjoy the outdoors, but don't let it derail your dental routine. Pack your toothbrush, toothpaste, and floss, and make sure to brush after meals even when you're away from home.

Use bottled or purified water for brushing if the campground water source is questionable. Stick to tooth-friendly snacks like nuts, cheese, and vegetables to protect your smile in the wild.
item.excerpt:encoded: Keep your dental routine on track during camping with these simple oral health tips.

item.title: Dangers of Thumb Sucking
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Thumb sucking is a natural reflex for infants and toddlers, but the habit can cause dental problems if it persists beyond age four or five. Prolonged thumb sucking can push teeth out of alignment, alter the shape of the jaw, and affect speech development.

If your child is still sucking their thumb after their permanent teeth begin to come in, talk to Dr. Cimo about gentle strategies to help break the habit.
item.excerpt:encoded: Prolonged thumb sucking can cause dental problems. Learn when to be concerned.

item.title: Are My Child’s Baby Teeth on Schedule?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Most babies get their first tooth between 6 and 12 months of age, with all 20 primary teeth typically in place by age three. Every child develops at their own pace, but significant delays may warrant a dental checkup.

Regular dental visits starting around your child's first birthday allow Dr. Cimo to monitor tooth development and address any concerns early. Schedule your child's visit at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn about typical baby tooth development timelines and when to consult a dentist.

item.title: Five Easy-to-Keep Dental Resolutions for the New Year
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
New Year's resolutions often fall apart by February, but dental resolutions are easy to keep when you start small. Commit to flossing daily, replacing your toothbrush every three months, cutting back on sugary drinks, scheduling your biannual cleanings, and wearing your night guard if you grind your teeth.

Small consistent habits make a big difference over time. Let Britt Moore Dental help you build a healthier smile this year.
item.excerpt:encoded: Five simple dental resolutions you can actually keep this year for a healthier smile.

item.title: What Do I Do If I Fall and Loosen My Teeth?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A fall that loosens your teeth is a dental emergency. Don't wiggle or push on loose teeth — call your dentist immediately. If a tooth has been knocked out, handle it by the crown (not the root), rinse gently with water, and try to reinsert it into the socket or store it in milk on your way to the office.

Britt Moore Dental treats dental emergencies promptly. Call us right away if you experience trauma to your teeth.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn what to do immediately if a fall loosens or knocks out your teeth.

item.title: Why Do Wisdom Teeth Need to Be Removed?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Wisdom teeth, or third molars, often don't have enough room to emerge properly and can become impacted, causing pain, infection, and damage to neighboring teeth. That's why many dentists recommend removing them before problems arise.

Not everyone needs their wisdom teeth removed — your dentist will evaluate your X-rays and jaw structure to make a recommendation. Contact Britt Moore Dental if you're experiencing wisdom tooth pain.
item.excerpt:encoded: Understanding why wisdom teeth often need to be removed and what the process involves.

item.title: Let’s Talk About Your Roots
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The roots of your teeth anchor them to the jawbone and house nerves and blood vessels. When decay or infection reaches the root, you may need root canal therapy to save the tooth and relieve pain.

Root canals have an undeserved reputation for being painful — in reality, modern techniques make the procedure comfortable. Ask Dr. Cimo about root canal therapy at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn about tooth roots, root canal therapy, and what to expect from the procedure.

item.title: Holiday Gift Ideas for Healthy Holiday Smiles
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Looking for a thoughtful gift that promotes good health? Consider an electric toothbrush, a water flosser, whitening strips, or a gift card to a dental office. These practical gifts show you care about the recipient's well-being.

Other tooth-friendly stocking stuffers include sugar-free gum, dental floss picks, and travel-size oral care kits. Spread some oral health cheer this holiday season!
item.excerpt:encoded: Thoughtful holiday gift ideas that promote good oral health for the whole family.

item.title: Tips for a Tooth-Friendly Thanksgiving
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Thanksgiving is full of tooth-friendly foods like turkey (high in protein and phosphorus), sweet potatoes (rich in vitamins), and cranberries (natural antioxidants). Enjoy these alongside plenty of water to rinse away food particles.

Watch out for sticky pies, hard candies, and excess alcohol, which can be harmful to your teeth and gums. Brush and floss after the feast, and have a wonderful holiday from all of us at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Enjoy Thanksgiving while keeping your smile healthy with these tooth-friendly tips.

item.title: The Importance of Baby Teeth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Baby teeth, or primary teeth, are vital for chewing, speaking, and guiding permanent teeth into proper position. Even though they eventually fall out, neglecting baby teeth can lead to early decay, pain, and problems with permanent teeth.

Start caring for your child's teeth as soon as they appear, and schedule their first dental visit by age one. Healthy baby teeth set the stage for a lifetime of healthy smiles.
item.excerpt:encoded: Baby teeth play a crucial role in your child's development. Learn why they deserve proper care.

item.title: Should Adults Continue Fluoride Treatments?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Fluoride is often associated with children's dentistry, but adults benefit from fluoride too. Adults who experience dry mouth, have exposed root surfaces, or are at high risk for cavities can greatly benefit from professional fluoride treatments.

Fluoride strengthens enamel and helps remineralize early decay. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental whether fluoride treatments are right for you at your next appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Fluoride isn't just for kids. Learn why adults can benefit from fluoride treatments too.

item.title: Tips for Cold and Flu Season
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Cold and flu season can take a toll on your oral health. Vomiting exposes teeth to stomach acid, sugary cough syrups can promote cavities, and dry mouth from breathing through your nose increases decay risk.

Replace your toothbrush after recovering from illness, stay hydrated, and choose sugar-free medications when possible. If you've been sick, schedule a checkup at Britt Moore Dental to make sure your teeth are in good shape.
item.excerpt:encoded: Protect your oral health during cold and flu season with these practical tips.

item.title: Let’s Make Halloween Treats Less Tricky
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Halloween candy doesn't have to be a dental disaster. The worst offenders are sticky, chewy candies that cling to teeth for hours. Better choices include chocolate, which rinses off more easily, and sugar-free options.

Set a time limit for candy consumption, encourage rinsing with water after treats, and make sure kids brush and floss before bed. Happy Halloween from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Smart Halloween candy choices can help protect your child's teeth this spooky season.

item.title: Anemia and Your Oral Health
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Anemia — a deficiency in red blood cells or hemoglobin — can manifest in your mouth. Symptoms such as pale gums, tongue soreness, mouth sores, and burning sensations may be early signs of anemia.

Your dentist can sometimes spot signs of anemia during a routine exam. If you notice unusual changes in your gums or tongue, mention them to Dr. Cimo at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Anemia can have oral health symptoms. Learn what signs to watch for in your mouth.

item.title: What Kind of Toothbrush and Toothpaste Should My Child Use?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
For children under three, use a soft-bristled toothbrush with a rice-grain-sized smear of fluoride toothpaste. From ages three to six, a pea-sized amount is appropriate. Choose a toothbrush with a small head and soft bristles designed for children.

Fun characters or colors can make brushing more appealing to kids. Ask Dr. Cimo for specific product recommendations at your child's next checkup at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Guidelines for choosing the right toothbrush and toothpaste for your child's age.

item.title: October Is Orthodontic Health Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
October is Orthodontic Health Month, a great time to consider whether orthodontic treatment could improve your smile. Properly aligned teeth are not only more attractive — they're easier to clean and less prone to problems like uneven wear and jaw pain.

Ask about orthodontic options including traditional braces and clear aligner therapy at Britt Moore Dental. A straighter smile is possible at any age.
item.excerpt:encoded: October is Orthodontic Health Month. Learn how aligned teeth benefit your overall oral health.

item.title: How Do I Clean My Baby’s Teeth?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Before your baby's first tooth appears, wipe their gums with a soft, damp cloth after feedings. Once teeth emerge, use a small, soft-bristled toothbrush and a tiny smear of fluoride toothpaste to clean them gently.

Start dental visits around your baby's first birthday to establish a healthy routine early. The team at Britt Moore Dental makes first visits fun and stress-free for babies and parents alike.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to clean your baby's teeth and gums from birth through toddlerhood.

item.title: Things You Should Know Before Getting an Oral Piercing
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Oral piercings carry significant risks for your dental health. Tongue and lip jewelry can chip or crack teeth, damage gums, cause excessive drooling, interfere with speech, and increase infection risk. Metal jewelry rubbing against gum tissue can cause gum recession.

If you already have an oral piercing, mention it at your next dental exam so Dr. Cimo can monitor for any related issues. Britt Moore Dental recommends caution before proceeding with any oral piercing.
item.excerpt:encoded: Know the dental risks before getting an oral piercing, including chipped teeth and gum damage.

item.title: Top Five Things to Keep Your Dentist Smiling
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Want to make your dentist happy? Show up on time, brush twice daily, floss every night, avoid smoking, and limit sugary drinks. These five habits are the foundation of outstanding oral health and make for very happy hygienists and dentists!

Of course, scheduling and attending your regular checkups is the most important thing you can do. Call Britt Moore Dental today to book your next appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Five simple habits your dentist loves — and that will keep your smile healthy for years.

item.title: The Stages of Gum Disease
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Gum disease progresses in stages. Gingivitis is the earliest and most reversible stage, characterized by red, swollen, bleeding gums. Left untreated, it can advance to periodontitis, which causes the gums to pull away from teeth and bone loss to occur.

Early intervention is key — regular cleanings and improved home care can reverse gingivitis. Contact Britt Moore Dental if your gums are bleeding or feel tender.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn about the stages of gum disease and why early treatment makes all the difference.

item.title: Keep Those Teeth Shipshape
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A healthy smile takes consistent effort, but the rewards are worth it. Brushing, flossing, eating well, staying hydrated, and seeing your dentist regularly are the pillars of excellent oral health at any age.

Think of your dental routine as routine maintenance — the more consistent you are, the better your results. The team at Britt Moore Dental is here to support your oral health journey every step of the way.
item.excerpt:encoded: Consistency is the secret to keeping your teeth healthy. Learn the pillars of great oral care.

item.title: Lip Service
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Your lips are the gateway to your oral health and deserve care too. Chapped or cracked lips can be caused by dehydration, sun exposure, or mouth breathing. Keep lips moisturized with a lip balm containing SPF to protect against sun damage and oral cancer risk.

Your dentist also examines lips and surrounding tissues during routine exams as part of an oral cancer screening. Schedule your next checkup at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Caring for your lips is part of comprehensive oral health. Learn what to watch for.

item.title: What Is a Water Pick and Do I Need One?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A water pick (or water flosser) uses a stream of pulsating water to remove food and plaque from between teeth and along the gumline. It can be especially useful for people with braces, dental implants, bridges, or those who find traditional flossing difficult.

While water flossers are a great supplement to flossing, they don't fully replace string floss for most people. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental whether a water flosser is right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn what a water flosser does and whether it can benefit your oral care routine.

item.title: Most Valuable Dental Treatments
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to dental value, preventive care wins every time. Regular cleanings, exams, and X-rays catch problems before they become expensive and complex. Dental sealants and fluoride treatments are also high-value investments for cavity prevention.

If you're looking to maximize your dental budget, prioritize prevention. Ask the team at Britt Moore Dental how to get the most out of your dental benefits this year.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover which dental treatments offer the greatest long-term value for your health and budget.

item.title: Sunbeams and Beaming Smiles
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer sunshine is great for your mood and vitamin D levels, but don't forget that UV radiation also affects your oral health. Sun exposure increases the risk of lip cancer, so always apply an SPF lip balm before heading outdoors.

Stay hydrated in the heat too — dry mouth increases cavity risk. Enjoy the sunshine safely, and keep smiling brightly with help from Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Protect your smile this summer with sun safety tips for your lips and oral health.

item.title: How Smoking Increases the Risk of Oral Cancer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Smoking is one of the leading risk factors for oral cancer. Tobacco use — including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, and smokeless tobacco — significantly increases your risk of developing cancers of the mouth, tongue, lips, and throat.

Regular oral cancer screenings are essential for smokers and former smokers. At Britt Moore Dental, we perform thorough oral cancer screenings as part of every exam. Quitting smoking is the single best thing you can do for your oral and overall health.
item.excerpt:encoded: Smoking dramatically increases oral cancer risk. Learn why screenings are critical for smokers.

item.title: Make Brushing Teeth Fun
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Getting kids to brush their teeth doesn't have to be a battle. Try letting them pick their own toothbrush featuring a favorite character, playing a two-minute song, using a brushing timer app, or making it a family activity where everyone brushes together.

Positive reinforcement and consistency help establish brushing as a lifelong habit. Ask Dr. Cimo for more child-friendly oral health tips at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Creative strategies to make brushing teeth an enjoyable habit for children.

item.title: Happy Gums, Happy Heart
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Research increasingly links gum health to heart health. Bacteria from periodontal disease can enter the bloodstream and contribute to inflammation, which is associated with heart disease, stroke, and other cardiovascular problems.

Taking care of your gums is one of the best investments you can make in your overall health. Schedule a periodontal evaluation at Britt Moore Dental if you haven't had one recently.
item.excerpt:encoded: The connection between gum health and heart health is stronger than you might think.

item.title: Four Great Additions to Your Dental-Healthy Diet
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
What you eat matters for your teeth. Dairy products like cheese and yogurt provide calcium that strengthens enamel. Crunchy vegetables like carrots and celery act as natural toothbrushes. Leafy greens are rich in vitamins that support gum health. Green tea contains compounds that fight bacteria.

Incorporating these foods into your diet is a delicious way to support your oral health. Ask the team at Britt Moore Dental for more dietary tips at your next visit.
item.excerpt:encoded: Four tooth-friendly foods you should be eating more of for a healthier smile.

item.title: Celebrate the Fourth with a Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Fourth of July celebrations are full of great food and fun, but be mindful of tooth-unfriendly treats like popcorn, sticky BBQ ribs, hard candies, and ice (never chew ice!). Opt for water over sugary sodas and sports drinks to stay hydrated without harming your enamel.

Have a wonderful Independence Day from everyone at Britt Moore Dental. Come see us after the holiday for your regular checkup!
item.excerpt:encoded: Enjoy your Fourth of July celebrations while keeping your smile healthy with these tips.

item.title: Oral Health for the Young Adult
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Young adults often feel invincible, but oral health problems can quietly develop during these years. Wisdom teeth, gum disease, and the effects of diet and lifestyle choices all become more relevant in your 20s and 30s.

Don't let a busy schedule push dental visits to the back burner. Regular checkups are just as important in young adulthood as at any other age. Book your appointment at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Why young adults need to prioritize dental care and what issues to watch for.

item.title: What Are the Five Things I Should Do In Between Visits?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Between dental visits, five habits will keep your smile in great shape: brush thoroughly twice daily, floss every night before bed, rinse with a fluoride mouthwash, limit acidic and sugary foods and drinks, and stay well hydrated.

These simple steps significantly reduce your risk of cavities and gum disease. The team at Britt Moore Dental will review your home care routine at your next checkup and offer personalized tips.
item.excerpt:encoded: Five essential habits to maintain between dental visits for a healthy smile.

item.title: Minimally Invasive Dentistry
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Minimally invasive dentistry focuses on preserving as much natural tooth structure as possible while treating decay and disease. Modern techniques like air abrasion, laser dentistry, and early detection technology allow us to treat problems with less drilling and fewer invasive procedures.

At Britt Moore Dental, we embrace minimally invasive approaches that protect your natural teeth and keep treatments comfortable. Ask about our modern technology at your next visit.
item.excerpt:encoded: Minimally invasive dentistry preserves more of your natural tooth while treating decay effectively.

item.title: Summer Treats for Healthy Teeth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer is full of sweet temptations, but many seasonal treats can be enjoyed without guilt. Fresh berries, watermelon, and peaches are hydrating and far gentler on teeth than sticky candies or sugary ice cream. Greek yogurt parfaits and cheese plates make great tooth-friendly snacks.

When you do indulge in frozen treats, rinse with water afterward and wait 30 minutes before brushing. Enjoy your summer smile with care from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Enjoy summer while keeping your smile healthy with tooth-friendly treat alternatives.

item.title: Quit Smoking to Save Your Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Smoking stains teeth, causes bad breath, slows healing after dental procedures, dramatically increases the risk of gum disease, and is a leading cause of oral cancer. The good news is that quitting smoking can start to reverse many of these effects.

Your dental team can be a resource as you work to quit smoking. Talk to Dr. Cimo about cessation resources at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Quitting smoking is one of the best things you can do for your oral and overall health.

item.title: Five Common Reasons for Emergency Care Visits
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental emergencies often come without warning. The five most common reasons patients seek emergency dental care are: severe toothache, knocked-out tooth, broken or cracked tooth, lost filling or crown, and dental abscess. Each of these requires prompt professional attention.

Britt Moore Dental accommodates dental emergencies. If you're experiencing dental pain or trauma, call our office right away — we'll do our best to see you quickly.
item.excerpt:encoded: Know the five most common dental emergencies and what to do when they happen.

item.title: The Link Between HPV and Oral Cancer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Human papillomavirus (HPV) is now recognized as a significant risk factor for oropharyngeal cancers — cancers affecting the back of the throat, base of the tongue, and tonsils. HPV-related oral cancers are increasing, particularly in non-smoking adults.

Regular oral cancer screenings are important for everyone, regardless of smoking status. Britt Moore Dental performs oral cancer screenings as part of every comprehensive exam.
item.excerpt:encoded: HPV is increasingly linked to oral cancers. Learn why regular screenings matter for everyone.

item.title: May Marks National Physical Fitness and Sports Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Physical fitness and oral health go hand in hand. Exercise benefits your overall health, which in turn supports oral health. However, sports activities increase the risk of dental injuries, making mouth guards essential protective gear for contact sports.

Ask about custom-fitted mouth guards at Britt Moore Dental — they offer far superior protection compared to over-the-counter versions. Stay active, and protect your smile this sports season.
item.excerpt:encoded: Sports participation is great for health but requires proper dental protection. Learn more.

item.title: When to Replace Fillings
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental fillings don't last forever. Composite (tooth-colored) fillings typically last 7–10 years, while amalgam fillings may last longer. Signs that a filling needs replacing include cracking, sensitivity, visible wear, or the sensation that your bite feels off.

Your dentist will monitor your fillings at each checkup and let you know when replacement is recommended. Schedule your exam at Britt Moore Dental to make sure your fillings are in good shape.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how long dental fillings last and the signs that indicate it's time for a replacement.

item.title: The Importance of Regular Dental Checkups
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Regular dental checkups every six months are the cornerstone of preventive oral care. These visits allow your dentist to detect cavities, gum disease, and oral cancer in their earliest and most treatable stages — often before you notice any symptoms.

A professional cleaning removes tartar buildup that brushing and flossing can't address at home. Don't wait until something hurts — schedule your next checkup at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Regular dental checkups catch problems early and keep your smile healthy year-round.

item.title: When Should I Floss During the Day?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The best time to floss is at night before bed, after your last meal. This removes food and plaque that has accumulated throughout the day, preventing bacteria from feeding on those particles overnight when saliva flow is reduced.

If you can only floss once a day, bedtime is the optimal time. Consistency matters more than timing — the important thing is that you floss every single day.
item.excerpt:encoded: When is the best time to floss? Learn why nighttime flossing is ideal for your oral health.

item.title: The Importance of Oral Cancer Screenings
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Oral cancer affects tens of thousands of Americans each year, and early detection dramatically improves survival rates. Oral cancer screenings are quick, painless, and performed as part of your regular dental exam at Britt Moore Dental.

Risk factors include tobacco use, heavy alcohol consumption, HPV, and prolonged sun exposure to the lips. Everyone should receive regular screenings — don't skip your checkup.
item.excerpt:encoded: Early detection through oral cancer screenings can be life-saving. Learn more about the process.

item.title: Snowball Effect
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental problems have a snowball effect — small issues left untreated grow into bigger, more expensive problems. A cavity that could be filled with a small restoration may require a crown if it deepens, or even a root canal if infection sets in.

The best way to stop the snowball is through regular preventive care. Don't let small problems grow into large ones — schedule your checkup at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Small dental problems grow into big ones when left untreated. The case for regular preventive care.

item.title: Dental Anxiety
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental anxiety is a real and common concern that causes many people to avoid necessary dental care. Whether your anxiety stems from fear of pain, past negative experiences, or a sense of loss of control, there are effective strategies to help.

At Britt Moore Dental, we offer a compassionate approach and multiple sedation options to make your visit comfortable. Talk to us about your concerns — we're here to help.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental anxiety is common and treatable. Learn how our team can help you feel at ease.

item.title: Brushing Tips for Kids
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Teaching children to brush properly takes patience and practice. Use a soft-bristled brush, a pea-sized amount of fluoride toothpaste, and gentle circular motions. Brush all surfaces — outer, inner, and chewing — for a full two minutes.

Children need parental help with brushing until around age 7–8. Make brushing a positive routine with songs, timers, or apps. Britt Moore Dental is happy to demonstrate proper technique at your child's next visit.
item.excerpt:encoded: Help your child learn proper brushing technique with these age-by-age tips.

item.title: St. Patrick’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Happy St. Patrick's Day from Britt Moore Dental! While you celebrate with green-colored treats and festive foods, remember that highly pigmented foods and beverages can stain your teeth. Green beer, in particular, combines enamel-staining alcohol with coloring agents.

Rinse with water after consuming staining foods and drinks, and consider a professional whitening treatment if discoloration is a concern. We'd love to see you for a brighter smile this spring!
item.excerpt:encoded: St. Patrick's Day greetings and tips to protect your smile from holiday staining.

item.title: How Sedation Dentistry Can Help You Overcome Dental Anxiety
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sedation dentistry offers a way for anxious patients to receive the dental care they need in a relaxed, comfortable state. Options range from nitrous oxide (laughing gas) for mild anxiety to oral conscious sedation and IV sedation for deeper relaxation.

With sedation, multiple procedures can often be completed in fewer visits, and patients frequently remember little or nothing of the appointment. Contact Britt Moore Dental to learn which sedation option is right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Sedation dentistry helps anxious patients get the dental care they need comfortably.

item.title: I Haven’t Been to the Dentist in Years — What Should I Expect?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
If it's been years since your last dental visit, you may feel nervous about what you'll find — but the best time to come back is now. Your dentist will take X-rays, perform a thorough exam, and complete a deep cleaning to get you back on track.

At Britt Moore Dental, we never judge patients for gaps in care. We're here to help you start fresh. Call us today to schedule your long-overdue checkup — we'll take care of you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Returning to the dentist after years away doesn't have to be scary. Here's what to expect.

item.title: Is It Possible to Over-Brush?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Yes — brushing too hard or too frequently can damage your enamel and cause gum recession. Use a soft-bristled brush, apply gentle pressure, and brush for two minutes twice daily. Aggressive scrubbing is not more effective and can harm your teeth over time.

If your gums appear to be receding or your teeth feel sensitive, mention it at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental. Your hygienist can demonstrate the proper brushing technique.
item.excerpt:encoded: Too much brushing can actually damage your teeth. Learn the right technique and frequency.

item.title: The Origins of Valentine’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Valentine's Day has ancient roots, blending traditions from Roman festivals and Christian feast days. Today it's associated with romance, chocolates, and heart-shaped treats. From a dental standpoint, chocolates are actually a better choice than sticky candies for your teeth.

Celebrate love and share dark chocolate this Valentine's Day — your smile will thank you. Happy Valentine's Day from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: A brief history of Valentine's Day and dental-friendly treat suggestions for the holiday.

item.title: Educational and Entertaining Enamel Experiments
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Teaching kids about tooth enamel doesn't have to be boring. Simple at-home experiments — like soaking a hard-boiled egg in cola to show acid erosion, or testing different liquids for their pH — make dental education fun and memorable.

Understanding why we protect our enamel helps children make better food choices. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental for more kid-friendly dental education ideas.
item.excerpt:encoded: Fun science experiments to teach kids about enamel protection and dental health.

item.title: What Is a Cavity?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A cavity, also called tooth decay or caries, is a permanently damaged area in the hard surface of a tooth that develops into a tiny opening or hole. Cavities are caused by bacteria in plaque producing acids that erode enamel over time.

Untreated cavities grow larger and can affect deeper layers of the tooth. Treatment with a dental filling restores the tooth and stops the decay. Schedule regular checkups at Britt Moore Dental for early detection.
item.excerpt:encoded: What causes cavities, how they develop, and how dental fillings restore damaged teeth.

item.title: Medication Can Lead to Xerostomia in Women
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Xerostomia, or chronic dry mouth, is a common side effect of hundreds of medications including antidepressants, antihistamines, and blood pressure drugs. Women are more likely to take multiple medications and experience dry mouth as a result.

Dry mouth significantly increases cavity risk because saliva plays a crucial role in neutralizing acids and washing away food particles. If you experience dry mouth, tell Dr. Cimo — there are effective management strategies available.
item.excerpt:encoded: Many medications cause dry mouth (xerostomia), which can increase your cavity risk significantly.

item.title: Periodontics and Braces Treatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Orthodontic treatment and periodontal health go hand in hand. Gum disease can complicate or even prevent orthodontic treatment, and braces make thorough cleaning more challenging — increasing the risk of gum problems during treatment.

Patients with gum disease should address it before beginning orthodontic treatment. The team at Britt Moore Dental works with orthodontists to coordinate comprehensive care for the best possible outcomes.
item.excerpt:encoded: Why periodontal health matters before and during orthodontic treatment with braces.

item.title: Tooth Discoloration: Common Causes and What You Can Do to Stop It
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth discoloration can result from coffee, tea, red wine, tobacco, certain medications, aging, or trauma. Surface stains can often be removed with professional cleaning and whitening, while intrinsic discoloration (inside the tooth) may require veneers or bonding.

Prevention includes limiting staining foods and drinks, rinsing with water after consuming them, and maintaining regular cleanings. Ask about whitening options at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Common causes of tooth discoloration and effective solutions from prevention to professional whitening.

item.title: Resolving to Eat Better in the New Year
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A healthy diet benefits your whole body — including your teeth. This New Year, resolve to eat more calcium-rich dairy products, crunchy fruits and vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains. Cut back on sugary snacks, acidic beverages, and sticky foods that promote cavities.

Your dental team at Britt Moore Dental can offer personalized dietary guidance to support your oral health goals this year and beyond.
item.excerpt:encoded: New Year's resolution tip: improve your diet for better oral and overall health.

item.title: Teeth Grinding: Not Just a Bad Habit, But a Dental Concern
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Bruxism (teeth grinding) is more than just a bad habit — it can cause significant damage to your teeth, jaw, and joints over time. Symptoms include worn enamel, jaw pain, headaches, and tooth sensitivity. Grinding often happens during sleep, so you may not realize you're doing it.

A custom night guard from Britt Moore Dental can protect your teeth from grinding damage. Schedule an evaluation if you suspect you grind your teeth.
item.excerpt:encoded: Teeth grinding (bruxism) can cause real dental damage. Learn the signs and how a night guard helps.

item.title: Why Do Wisdom Teeth Need to Be Removed?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Most people don't have enough room in their jaws for wisdom teeth to erupt properly. When wisdom teeth become impacted — stuck beneath the gumline — they can cause infection, damage neighboring teeth, and significant pain.

Early removal, often in the late teens or early twenties, is generally easier and involves faster recovery. If you're experiencing wisdom tooth discomfort, call Britt Moore Dental for an evaluation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Why wisdom teeth often need removal and why earlier is better for the extraction procedure.

item.title: Is Periodontal Disease Genetic?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Research suggests genetics play a role in periodontal disease susceptibility. If you have a family history of gum disease, you may be at higher risk even with excellent oral hygiene. Genetic factors can affect how aggressively your immune system responds to bacterial infection.

Knowing your family history helps your dentist personalize your preventive care plan. Tell Dr. Cimo about any family history of gum disease at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Genetics can influence your risk for gum disease. Learn what this means for your oral care.

item.title: Snack Attack
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Frequent snacking throughout the day gives bacteria more opportunities to produce acid and attack your enamel. Each snack resets the acid attack clock. Limiting snacks to set times and choosing tooth-friendly options like cheese, nuts, and raw vegetables can significantly reduce your cavity risk.

Rinse with water after snacking if you can't brush. Your teeth will thank you for smarter snacking habits — and so will your dentist at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: How frequent snacking affects your enamel and what tooth-friendly snack choices to make.

item.title: Safety of Dental X-Ray Radiation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental X-rays use a very small amount of radiation — far less than a cross-country flight. Modern digital X-ray technology has reduced radiation exposure by up to 80% compared to traditional film X-rays. Lead aprons and thyroid collars provide additional protection.

The diagnostic benefits of X-rays far outweigh the minimal risk. At Britt Moore Dental, we use state-of-the-art digital X-ray equipment and only take X-rays when clinically necessary.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental X-rays are safe — modern digital technology uses minimal radiation. Here's why they're important.

item.title: Let’s Talk About Fluoride
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Fluoride is a naturally occurring mineral that strengthens tooth enamel and helps reverse early stages of tooth decay. It works by remineralizing areas where acid has begun to erode the enamel surface.

You can get fluoride through fluoridated water, fluoride toothpaste, and professional fluoride treatments at your dental office. Ask about professional fluoride applications at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Fluoride strengthens enamel and prevents cavities. Learn how to ensure you're getting enough.

item.title: Is Coffee Damaging Your Smile?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Coffee is one of the most common causes of tooth staining. Its dark pigments and mild acidity can gradually discolor enamel over time. Adding milk or drinking through a straw reduces contact time with teeth.

Regular professional cleanings can remove coffee stains, and whitening treatments can restore your smile's brightness. Enjoy your coffee — just be mindful of its effects on your teeth, and see us at Britt Moore Dental regularly.
item.excerpt:encoded: Coffee can stain and damage your enamel. Learn how to enjoy it while protecting your smile.

item.title: National Brush Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
National Brush Day is celebrated on November 1st — the day after Halloween — as a reminder to brush away all that holiday candy. It's a perfect opportunity to reinforce good brushing habits with your children.

Use National Brush Day as a springboard to establish a consistent brushing routine. Two minutes, twice a day, with fluoride toothpaste. Your smile will thank you year-round!
item.excerpt:encoded: National Brush Day reminds us all — especially kids — to brush away Halloween candy.

item.title: Protect Your Baby from Early Childhood Caries
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Early childhood caries (baby bottle tooth decay) is one of the most common chronic conditions in young children. It is caused by prolonged exposure to sugary liquids — including formula, milk, and juice — especially during sleep when saliva flow decreases.

Never put your baby to bed with a bottle, and transition to a sippy cup around age one. Schedule your child's first dental visit at Britt Moore Dental by their first birthday.
item.excerpt:encoded: Prevent baby bottle tooth decay with these simple habits for your infant's oral health.

item.title: What’s the Best Dental Floss?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The best dental floss is the one you'll actually use consistently. Traditional string floss, floss picks, interdental brushes, and water flossers all effectively clean between teeth when used correctly. Waxed floss slides more easily between tight contacts, while wider tape floss works well for wider spaces.

Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental for a personalized flossing product recommendation based on your tooth spacing and gum health.
item.excerpt:encoded: The best dental floss is one you use every day. Learn which type suits your needs.

item.title: What to Do When Your Child Has a Loose Tooth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A loose baby tooth is usually an exciting milestone! Encourage your child to wiggle it gently with clean hands until it falls out naturally. Avoid pulling it out forcefully — if it's not ready, it can be painful and may cause bleeding.

If a permanent tooth is loose due to injury or decay, that requires prompt dental attention. Call Britt Moore Dental right away if your child has a loose permanent tooth.
item.excerpt:encoded: What to do — and not do — when your child has a loose baby tooth.

item.title: When to Begin Dental Care for Your Baby
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental care begins before your baby's first tooth appears. Gently wipe your newborn's gums with a soft, damp cloth after feedings to remove bacteria. When the first tooth appears — typically between 6 and 12 months — start brushing with an infant toothbrush.

The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry recommends the first dental visit by age one. Schedule your baby's first appointment at Britt Moore Dental to start them on a lifetime of healthy smiles.
item.excerpt:encoded: Start dental care before your baby's first tooth even appears. Here's how and when to begin.

item.title: Year-End Insurance Reminder
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Most dental insurance plans reset on January 1st — meaning any unused benefits expire at year's end. If you haven't used your annual maximum, now is the time to schedule any outstanding dental work before those benefits are lost.

Don't leave money on the table. Call Britt Moore Dental before December 31st to schedule treatments that can be covered by your expiring benefits. Your smile and your wallet will thank you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Use your dental insurance benefits before they expire at year's end. Don't leave money on the table.

item.title: Understanding Dental Insurance Terminology
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental insurance terminology can be confusing. Key terms to know: premium (monthly cost), deductible (amount you pay before insurance kicks in), annual maximum (most insurance will pay per year), copay (your share per visit), and in-network (dentists contracted with your insurer).

Our team at Britt Moore Dental is happy to help you understand your benefits and maximize your coverage. Call us with any insurance questions before your appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: A plain-language guide to understanding dental insurance terms and maximizing your benefits.

item.title: Choosing the Dental Filling Option That’s Best for You
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Today's dental filling options include tooth-colored composite resin, durable ceramic, and traditional amalgam. Composite fillings blend seamlessly with natural teeth and are the most popular choice for visible areas. They require slightly more tooth removal and may not last as long as amalgam in high-pressure areas.

Dr. Cimo will recommend the best filling material based on the cavity's location and size. Discuss your options at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Compare tooth-colored composite vs. amalgam fillings to choose the best option for your cavity.

item.title: Oh No, Not the Dentist!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Does your child dread the dentist? You're not alone — many children experience dental anxiety. The good news is that with the right approach, dental visits can become a positive experience your child looks forward to.

At Britt Moore Dental, we specialize in creating a welcoming, child-friendly environment. We explain everything in age-appropriate terms and take extra time to help anxious children feel at ease. Schedule your child's visit today!
item.excerpt:encoded: Tips for helping anxious children feel comfortable at dental appointments.

item.title: Football Season: Practice Dental Defense
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Football season means cheering for your team — and protecting your smile. Whether playing or watching, football season brings dental risks: players face mouth injuries, while fans may grind teeth in excitement or snack on game-day foods all day long.

Athletes should always wear a properly fitted mouth guard. Ask about custom sports guards at Britt Moore Dental — they offer significantly better protection than store-bought options.
item.excerpt:encoded: Football season calls for dental defense. Learn about custom mouth guards and game-day oral health.

item.title: Understanding Cavities
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Cavities form when bacteria in your mouth convert sugars and starches into acids that erode tooth enamel. Over time, the erosion creates holes in the teeth. Cavities can affect anyone — from toddlers to seniors — and are entirely preventable with proper oral hygiene.

Treatment depends on severity: small cavities need fillings; larger ones may need crowns; infected teeth may need root canals. Catch them early at Britt Moore Dental with regular checkups.
item.excerpt:encoded: How cavities form, how they progress, and how to prevent and treat them effectively.

item.title: Radiation and the Safety of Dental X-Rays
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The radiation dose from dental X-rays is extremely small — comparable to the background radiation you receive during a short plane flight. Modern digital X-ray technology uses up to 80% less radiation than traditional film X-rays.

The information gained from dental X-rays is invaluable for diagnosing problems that aren't visible to the naked eye. Protective lead aprons are used for all X-ray procedures at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental X-rays are extremely safe. Learn about radiation levels and protective measures we take.

item.title: Why Is My Child Getting Cavities?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Children are prone to cavities for several reasons: sugary diets, inconsistent brushing, deep molar grooves that trap food, and less enamel mineral density than adults. Bacteria can also be transmitted from parents to children through sharing utensils or kissing.

Dental sealants, fluoride treatments, and dietary changes can dramatically reduce your child's cavity risk. Talk to Dr. Cimo at Britt Moore Dental about a prevention plan tailored to your child.
item.excerpt:encoded: Why children are prone to cavities and what you can do to significantly reduce their risk.

item.title: What Is Hand, Foot, and Mouth Disease?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Hand, foot, and mouth disease is a common viral illness in young children that causes mouth sores, rash on hands and feet, and fever. The oral sores can make eating and swallowing painful, causing children to refuse food and fluids.

The condition typically resolves in 7–10 days. Keep your child hydrated with cool fluids and soft foods. If you have concerns about your child's oral symptoms, call Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: What parents need to know about hand, foot, and mouth disease and its oral symptoms.

item.title: The Consequences of Sports and Energy Drinks
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sports and energy drinks are highly acidic and often loaded with sugar, making them particularly damaging to tooth enamel. Studies have shown these drinks cause significantly more enamel erosion than sodas. Regular consumption can lead to increased tooth sensitivity and cavities.

Water is the ideal hydration choice. If you do consume sports drinks, rinse with water immediately after and wait at least 30 minutes before brushing. Ask about enamel protection at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Sports and energy drinks are more damaging to teeth than soda. Learn healthier hydration alternatives.

item.title: Dentin Tooth Sensitivity Treatments
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth sensitivity occurs when dentin — the layer beneath enamel — becomes exposed due to enamel erosion or gum recession. Hot, cold, sweet, or acidic foods and drinks can trigger sharp, temporary pain in sensitive teeth.

Treatments include desensitizing toothpaste, fluoride applications, bonding agents, and gum grafts for severe recession. Don't just live with tooth pain — schedule an evaluation at Britt Moore Dental to find the right solution for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Effective treatments for tooth sensitivity, from desensitizing toothpaste to professional procedures.

item.title: Fewer Adults Are Visiting the Dentist
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Studies show that fewer adults are keeping up with regular dental visits, often citing cost, fear, or busy schedules. This trend is concerning because oral diseases that could be caught early are going undetected until they become painful and expensive.

At Britt Moore Dental, we offer flexible scheduling, financing options, and a welcoming atmosphere to make dental care accessible and comfortable. Your health is worth prioritizing — call us today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Adults are skipping dental visits at alarming rates. Here's why you should make yours a priority.

item.title: Amalgam Fillings vs. White Fillings
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Amalgam (silver) fillings have been used for over 150 years and are known for durability. Composite (white) fillings match your natural tooth color and require less removal of healthy tooth structure. Both are safe and effective — the best choice depends on the cavity's location and size.

Many patients choose composite fillings for aesthetic reasons, especially for visible teeth. Ask Dr. Cimo which option is best for your specific situation at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Compare amalgam and tooth-colored composite fillings to find the best option for your needs.

item.title: Summer Dental Health: Get Into the Swim of It
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer swimming is a fun way to stay cool and active, but pool water can affect your teeth. Chlorinated pool water is acidic and can erode enamel with prolonged exposure. Swimmers who spend many hours in the pool may notice increased sensitivity or discoloration.

Rinse your mouth with fresh water after swimming, and mention your swimming habits to Dr. Cimo if you experience sensitivity. Enjoy your summer safely at Britt Moore Dental's recommendation!
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how swimming pool chlorine can affect your teeth and how to protect your smile.

item.title: Sleep Apnea: What a Dentist Can Do
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Many people don't realize that dentists can help treat sleep apnea. For mild to moderate obstructive sleep apnea, a custom oral appliance worn during sleep can reposition the jaw and tongue to keep the airway open — without a CPAP machine.

If you snore loudly, wake up tired, or have been diagnosed with sleep apnea, ask Dr. Cimo about oral appliance therapy at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dentists can help treat mild to moderate sleep apnea with custom oral appliance therapy.

item.title: Avoid the Emergency Room for Dental Problems
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Emergency rooms are not equipped to treat most dental problems. ER doctors can prescribe antibiotics and pain medication, but they can't fill a cavity, perform a root canal, or make a crown. An ER visit for a toothache typically costs significantly more than a dental visit.

Call Britt Moore Dental first for dental emergencies — we accommodate urgent cases and can provide real dental treatment, not just temporary pain relief.
item.excerpt:encoded: Save time and money by calling your dentist first for dental emergencies, not the ER.

item.title: Dental-Healthy Snacks for Your School-Aged Child
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
What your child snacks on at school matters for their dental health. Great choices include: string cheese (calcium-rich), apple slices (natural cleansing action), celery sticks (crunchy and fibrous), whole grain crackers, and plain yogurt. These options are nourishing and much gentler on teeth than chips or candy.

Pack water instead of juice boxes, and include a small floss pick in your child's lunch bag. Britt Moore Dental supports your child's dental health every step of the way.
item.excerpt:encoded: Pack tooth-friendly school snacks that nourish your child without harming their teeth.

item.title: Summer Break: An Ideal Time for Wisdom Teeth Removal
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer break is a popular time for teenagers and college students to have their wisdom teeth removed. With no school or exams to worry about, patients can recover comfortably at home without missing classes or important commitments.

Recovery from wisdom tooth removal typically takes 3–7 days. Schedule your teen's evaluation at Britt Moore Dental early in the summer to allow plenty of time for a comfortable recovery.
item.excerpt:encoded: Why summer break is the perfect time for teens and college students to have wisdom teeth removed.

item.title: Root Canal Recovery
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
After a root canal, mild soreness and sensitivity are normal for a few days. Over-the-counter pain relievers usually manage discomfort effectively. Avoid chewing on the treated side until your permanent crown is placed.

Most patients return to normal activities the next day. If you experience severe pain, swelling, or fever after your root canal, contact Britt Moore Dental right away. We're here to ensure your recovery goes smoothly.
item.excerpt:encoded: What to expect after a root canal procedure and tips for a smooth, comfortable recovery.

item.title: Memorial Day: Parades, Remembrance, and the Unofficial Start of Summer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Memorial Day honors the men and women who have served and sacrificed for our country. It also marks the unofficial start of summer — a season full of outdoor gatherings, BBQs, and activities. As you celebrate, be mindful of tooth-unfriendly foods like hard candies and sticky BBQ sauces.

From all of us at Britt Moore Dental, happy Memorial Day. Thank you to all who have served. Enjoy a safe and healthy start to summer!
item.excerpt:encoded: A Memorial Day message from Britt Moore Dental with tips for the unofficial start of summer.

item.title: Love Your New Smile – Tell Us About It!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing more rewarding than hearing that a patient loves their smile transformation. Whether you've had whitening, veneers, implants, or a complete smile makeover, we'd love to hear your story!

Patient reviews and testimonials help other people who are looking for quality dental care find our practice. If you've had a great experience at Britt Moore Dental, please consider sharing it online. Your smile story could inspire someone else to take the first step.
item.excerpt:encoded: Share your smile transformation story — patient reviews help others find great dental care.

item.title: When to Begin Dental Care for Your Child
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The American Dental Association recommends that children first visit the dentist within six months of their first tooth appearing, or by their first birthday. Early visits establish a positive relationship with dental care and allow the dentist to monitor tooth development and provide preventive treatments.

Don't wait until there's a problem to bring your child to the dentist. Schedule your child's first visit at Britt Moore Dental for a fun, welcoming introduction to dental care.
item.excerpt:encoded: When should your child first see a dentist? The answer might be earlier than you think.

item.title: Is Soda Really Bad for Your Teeth?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Yes — soda is one of the worst beverages for your teeth. It contains both sugar (which feeds cavity-causing bacteria) and phosphoric or citric acids (which erode enamel directly). Even diet sodas are acidic and can damage enamel despite containing no sugar.

If you drink soda, use a straw, rinse with water afterward, and avoid brushing for 30 minutes after to prevent spreading acid across your teeth. Better yet, choose water! Call Britt Moore Dental for more dietary guidance.
item.excerpt:encoded: Soda damages teeth through both sugar and acid. Learn smarter beverage choices for your smile.

item.title: Improve Your Oral Health with Xylitol
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Xylitol is a natural sugar substitute that actually fights cavities rather than causing them. Bacteria in the mouth cannot metabolize xylitol, which disrupts the acid production cycle that leads to decay. Chewing xylitol gum after meals can reduce cavity-causing bacteria.

Look for toothpaste, mints, and gum that contain xylitol as a primary sweetener. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental about incorporating xylitol products into your oral care routine.
item.excerpt:encoded: Xylitol fights cavities rather than causing them. Learn how to incorporate it into your routine.

item.title: April Is Oral Cancer Awareness Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
April is Oral Cancer Awareness Month. Over 50,000 Americans are diagnosed with oral cancer each year, yet the five-year survival rate when caught early is around 80%. Regular dental exams include oral cancer screenings that can detect suspicious tissue changes early.

Risk factors include tobacco use, heavy alcohol consumption, HPV, and sun exposure. Even with no risk factors, everyone should receive regular screenings. Schedule yours at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: April is Oral Cancer Awareness Month. Early detection through screenings saves lives.

item.title: Be Prepared
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Being prepared for dental emergencies can make all the difference. Keep the phone number of Britt Moore Dental saved in your phone. Know the basics: if a tooth is knocked out, handle it by the crown, keep it moist, and call immediately. For a toothache, rinse with warm salt water and call your dentist.

A dental first-aid kit with gauze, a tooth preservation kit, and pain reliever can also help in a pinch. Don't wait — call us right away in any dental emergency.
item.excerpt:encoded: How to be prepared for dental emergencies with basic first-aid knowledge and quick action.

item.title: Can I Use Mouthwash Instead of Flossing?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Mouthwash and flossing serve different purposes. Flossing physically removes plaque and food debris from between teeth — where your toothbrush can't reach. Mouthwash rinses the mouth and can reduce bacteria, freshen breath, and deliver fluoride, but it cannot scrape plaque off tooth surfaces.

Think of mouthwash as a complement to flossing, not a replacement. The most effective oral hygiene routine includes brushing, flossing, and mouthwash. Ask your hygienist for recommendations at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Mouthwash cannot replace flossing. Learn what each does and why both matter for your oral health.

item.title: Are Your Teeth Ready for the Big Day?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Getting married or attending a major event? Your smile will be in countless photos, so give it some extra attention beforehand. Professional teeth whitening, a cleaning, and bonding repairs can be completed in as little as two appointments to get your smile photo-ready.

Plan ahead — allow at least 4–6 weeks before your big day for whitening treatments. Schedule a smile consultation at Britt Moore Dental to discuss your options.
item.excerpt:encoded: Get your smile photo-ready before your wedding or big event with professional cosmetic treatments.

item.title: Be Good to Your Gums
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Healthy gums are the foundation of a healthy mouth. Gum disease affects nearly half of American adults and is the leading cause of tooth loss. But it's largely preventable with proper brushing, flossing, and regular professional cleanings.

Signs of gum disease include bleeding when brushing, swollen or red gums, persistent bad breath, and receding gum tissue. If you notice any of these, call Britt Moore Dental for an evaluation right away.
item.excerpt:encoded: Healthy gums support healthy teeth. Learn the signs of gum disease and how to prevent it.

item.title: Implants: Why It’s Important to Replace Missing Teeth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When a tooth is lost, the jawbone in that area begins to resorb without stimulation from a tooth root. Neighboring teeth may drift into the gap, altering your bite and making remaining teeth harder to clean. Dental implants replace the root as well as the crown, preventing bone loss and preserving facial structure.

Dental implants are the most natural-feeling, permanent tooth replacement option available. Ask Dr. Cimo whether you're a candidate at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Replacing missing teeth preserves bone and facial structure. Dental implants are the gold standard.

item.title: Which Whitening Is Right for You?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Teeth whitening options range from over-the-counter strips and toothpastes to in-office procedures like Zoom! whitening. Professional treatments are more effective and faster, with results that can lighten teeth by several shades in a single visit. Custom take-home trays from your dentist offer a middle ground.

Not all stains respond to whitening — intrinsic discoloration may require veneers. Schedule a consultation at Britt Moore Dental to find the whitening solution best suited to your smile.
item.excerpt:encoded: Compare teeth whitening options from OTC strips to professional in-office treatments.

item.title: Osteoporosis and Dental Health
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Osteoporosis weakens bones throughout the body, including the jawbone that supports your teeth. Women with osteoporosis are at higher risk for tooth loss and may experience more rapid progression of gum disease. Some osteoporosis medications can also affect dental treatment planning.

Tell Dr. Cimo about any osteoporosis diagnosis and medications you take. Regular dental care is especially important for patients with osteoporosis. Schedule your checkup at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Osteoporosis affects jawbone health and tooth retention. Learn what patients need to know.

item.title: How Does a Tooth Decay?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth decay is a gradual process. Bacteria in plaque feed on sugars and produce acids that demineralize enamel. Over time, a white spot forms, then softens into a cavity. Left untreated, decay progresses through enamel into dentin (causing sensitivity) and eventually into the pulp (causing infection and severe pain).

Early-stage decay can sometimes be reversed with fluoride. Catching it at the cavity stage requires a filling. Regular exams at Britt Moore Dental catch decay as early as possible.
item.excerpt:encoded: Understanding the step-by-step process of how tooth decay forms and progresses.

item.title: Broken Tooth: Is It an Emergency or Not?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Whether a broken tooth is an emergency depends on the severity. A small chip with no pain can usually wait a day or two for a non-emergency appointment. But if there's significant pain, a large break exposing the nerve, or sharp edges cutting your tongue, call your dentist immediately.

Never ignore a broken tooth — even painless breaks can harbor bacteria and worsen quickly. Call Britt Moore Dental to describe your situation and we'll advise the appropriate next step.
item.excerpt:encoded: When is a broken tooth a dental emergency? Learn the signs that require immediate attention.

item.title: My Mouth Is Dry — What Can I Do?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dry mouth (xerostomia) can be caused by medications, medical conditions, dehydration, or mouth breathing. Saliva is essential for oral health — it neutralizes acids, washes away food, and contains antibacterial compounds. Chronic dry mouth significantly increases cavity and gum disease risk.

Solutions include staying well hydrated, chewing sugar-free gum, using saliva substitutes, and addressing the underlying cause. Ask Dr. Cimo about managing dry mouth at your next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dry mouth affects your oral health significantly. Learn causes and effective management strategies.

item.title: Sleep Apnea: How We Can Help
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sleep apnea causes breathing to repeatedly stop and start during sleep, leading to poor sleep quality and serious health consequences including heart disease and high blood pressure. For patients with mild to moderate obstructive sleep apnea, a custom dental oral appliance can be an effective alternative to CPAP therapy.

Oral appliances reposition the jaw slightly to keep the airway open. Contact Britt Moore Dental if you or a partner snores loudly or you wake up frequently during the night.
item.excerpt:encoded: Custom oral appliances from your dentist can effectively treat mild to moderate sleep apnea.

item.title: Natural Ways to Soothe a Toothache
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
While a toothache always warrants a dental visit, natural remedies can provide temporary relief. Clove oil contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic. A warm salt water rinse can reduce inflammation. Over-the-counter pain relievers like ibuprofen can reduce pain and swelling until you can see your dentist.

Never ignore a toothache — it signals something that needs professional attention. Call Britt Moore Dental promptly to address the underlying cause of your tooth pain.
item.excerpt:encoded: Natural remedies for temporary toothache relief — but never delay calling your dentist.

item.title: November Marks National Diabetes Awareness Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Diabetes and oral health have a bidirectional relationship. Diabetes increases the risk of gum disease, dry mouth, slow healing, and oral infections. Conversely, untreated gum disease can make blood sugar control more difficult for diabetics.

If you have diabetes, maintaining excellent oral hygiene and scheduling more frequent dental visits is essential. Tell Dr. Cimo about your diabetes diagnosis and medication changes at every visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: The connection between diabetes and oral health — and why diabetics need more frequent dental care.

item.title: Finding the Right Dental Products for Your Child
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Walking down the dental aisle can be overwhelming for parents. Look for a toothbrush with soft bristles and a small head appropriate for your child's age. Choose a toothpaste with fluoride — the ADA seal of acceptance is a reliable indicator of safety and effectiveness.

Fun characters and flavors can help motivate reluctant brushers. Ask Dr. Cimo for age-specific product recommendations at your child's next visit to Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: A guide to choosing toothbrushes and toothpaste that are right for your child's age.

item.title: Caring for Your Night Guard
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Your night guard protects your teeth from grinding damage, but it needs regular care too. Rinse it with cold water after each use, brush gently with a soft toothbrush and mild soap, and allow it to air dry completely before storing. Never use hot water — it can warp the appliance.

Bring your night guard to every dental appointment so we can check it for wear. Replace it if it becomes thin or cracked. Contact Britt Moore Dental with any questions about your appliance.
item.excerpt:encoded: Proper care of your night guard extends its life and keeps it hygienic and effective.

item.title: Women’s Hormones and Oral Health
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Hormonal fluctuations throughout a woman's life — during puberty, menstruation, pregnancy, and menopause — can significantly affect oral health. Increased progesterone levels enhance blood flow to the gums, making them more susceptible to inflammation and bleeding.

Pregnancy gingivitis is common and should be monitored carefully. Women experiencing hormonal changes should mention them to Dr. Cimo and may benefit from more frequent cleanings. Schedule your visit at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Hormonal changes throughout a woman's life affect gum health. Learn what to watch for.

item.title: Treatments for Tooth Discoloration
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth discoloration treatments depend on the type and cause of staining. Extrinsic stains from food and drink respond well to professional cleaning and bleaching. Intrinsic stains (inside the tooth) from trauma, medication, or fluorosis may require veneers, bonding, or crowns for optimal results.

A smile consultation with Dr. Cimo at Britt Moore Dental will identify the type of discoloration and the most effective treatment options for your unique situation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Different types of tooth discoloration require different treatments. Find out what's right for you.

item.title: How Long Will a Root Canal Last?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
With proper care, a tooth treated with a root canal can last a lifetime. The key is protecting the treated tooth with a crown, which prevents fracture of the tooth structure that has become more brittle after the procedure. Good oral hygiene and regular dental visits are equally important.

Root canal-treated teeth can fail if re-infection occurs or the tooth cracks. Regular checkups at Britt Moore Dental ensure any issues are caught early to protect your investment.
item.excerpt:encoded: A root canal-treated tooth can last a lifetime with proper care. Here's how to protect it.

item.title: Pros and Cons of Veneers
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Porcelain veneers can transform your smile by covering chips, cracks, discoloration, gaps, and minor alignment issues. They're durable, stain-resistant, and look remarkably natural. The main downsides are cost, irreversibility (a small amount of enamel must be removed), and sensitivity that some patients experience after placement.

Veneers are a long-term investment in your smile. Schedule a smile makeover consultation at Britt Moore Dental to see if veneers are right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Porcelain veneers can transform your smile. Weigh the pros and cons before deciding.

item.title: Can Superfoods Create a Super Smile?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Superfoods like kale, spinach, almonds, salmon, and green tea offer real dental benefits. Leafy greens are rich in calcium and B vitamins that support gum health. Almonds are high in calcium and protein. Salmon provides vitamin D to help absorb calcium. Green tea contains catechins that suppress bacterial growth.

A diet rich in these foods supports your oral health from the inside out. Pair great nutrition with regular checkups at Britt Moore Dental for a super smile!
item.excerpt:encoded: Specific superfoods provide real dental benefits. Learn which ones to add to your diet.

item.title: Pick the Right Electric Toothbrush
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
