Electric toothbrushes are clinically proven to remove more plaque than manual brushes. When choosing one, look for an oscillating-rotating head design, a built-in two-minute timer, a pressure sensor to prevent over-brushing, and replacement heads that are readily available.

Both sonic and oscillating models are effective. The best one is the one you'll use consistently. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental for a recommendation at your next cleaning appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: How to choose the right electric toothbrush for better plaque removal and healthier gums.

item.title: What to Do About Sensitive Teeth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth sensitivity — sharp pain triggered by hot, cold, sweet, or acidic foods — affects millions of adults. Common causes include enamel erosion, exposed root surfaces from gum recession, cracked teeth, and recently placed fillings.

Don't just reach for sensitive toothpaste and hope for the best — sensitivity can signal a more serious underlying issue. Schedule an evaluation at Britt Moore Dental to identify the cause and get the right treatment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Tooth sensitivity deserves proper diagnosis, not just masking with special toothpaste.

item.title: TMD Problems and How You Can Prevent Them
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Temporomandibular disorders (TMD) cause pain and dysfunction in the jaw joint and muscles. Symptoms include jaw pain, clicking or popping sounds, headaches, earaches, and difficulty chewing. Bruxism (teeth grinding) is a common contributing factor.

Prevention includes stress reduction, avoiding hard or chewy foods, not chewing gum excessively, and wearing a night guard if you grind your teeth. Ask Dr. Cimo about TMD treatment options at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: TMD causes jaw pain and headaches. Learn what causes it and how to prevent and treat it.

item.title: A Variety of Dentures to Meet Your Needs
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Modern dentures are more comfortable and natural-looking than ever. Options include full dentures for patients missing all teeth, partial dentures that clip to remaining teeth, and implant-supported dentures for greater stability and bone preservation.

The right choice depends on how many teeth you've lost, your jawbone density, and your lifestyle preferences. Schedule a consultation at Britt Moore Dental to discuss which denture option best fits your needs and goals.
item.excerpt:encoded: From full to partial to implant-supported, explore the modern denture options available to you.

item.title: Preventing Periodontal Disease
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Periodontal disease is largely preventable with consistent oral hygiene and regular professional care. Brush twice daily, floss every night, avoid tobacco, maintain a healthy diet, and see your dentist every six months. Managing conditions like diabetes that increase risk is also important.

Catching gingivitis early — before it progresses to periodontitis — is key. Early treatment is simpler, less expensive, and more effective. Schedule your preventive care at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Periodontal disease is preventable. Learn the habits and professional care that keep gums healthy.

item.title: Aging and Dental Health
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
As we age, our dental needs change. Enamel wears down, gums recede slightly, root surfaces become exposed and vulnerable to decay, dry mouth from medications becomes more common, and arthritis can make brushing and flossing harder. These changes require adjustments to your dental care routine.

Regular dental visits remain as important as ever in older adulthood. The team at Britt Moore Dental provides personalized care that adapts to your changing needs as you age.
item.excerpt:encoded: How dental needs change with age and how to adapt your oral care routine accordingly.

item.title: Should I Use Mouthwash?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Mouthwash can be a valuable addition to your oral care routine. Antiseptic mouthwashes reduce bacteria and can help fight gum disease. Fluoride rinses strengthen enamel. However, mouthwash should supplement — not replace — brushing and flossing.

Choose the right type for your needs: anticavity fluoride rinse for cavity prevention, antibacterial for gum health, or whitening for mild stain removal. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental which type is best for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Should you add mouthwash to your routine? Learn the different types and their specific benefits.

item.title: Teeth Whitening for a Bright Summer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer is a season full of photos, events, and social gatherings — the perfect time to make sure your smile is at its brightest. Professional teeth whitening at Britt Moore Dental can lighten your smile by several shades in a single visit or with our convenient take-home whitening trays.

Safe, effective, and longer-lasting than over-the-counter options, professional whitening is a smart investment in your confidence. Call us today to schedule your whitening consultation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Get a brighter summer smile with professional teeth whitening from Britt Moore Dental.

item.title: Teeth Whitening for a Bright Summer (2)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Whether you're heading to the beach, attending a wedding, or just want to feel more confident, a brighter smile can make a real difference. Professional whitening provides safer, faster, and more predictable results than store-bought products.

Our whitening patients consistently report high satisfaction with their results. Schedule your whitening appointment at Britt Moore Dental and start your summer with a smile you love.
item.excerpt:encoded: Professional whitening delivers faster and safer results than over-the-counter options.

item.title: Teeth Whitening for a Bright Summer (3)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A bright white smile is one of the most requested cosmetic dental improvements. Summer is a great time to invest in whitening — you'll look and feel great for all those outdoor photos and social events.

At Britt Moore Dental, we offer both in-office whitening for instant results and custom take-home trays for gradual brightening at your convenience. Ask about our current whitening specials when you call.
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore in-office and take-home whitening options for a brighter smile this summer.

item.title: Losing a Baby Tooth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Losing baby teeth is an exciting milestone for children! Most children start losing teeth around age 6, beginning with the lower front teeth. The process continues until about age 12. Let loose teeth fall out naturally — wiggling is fine, but avoid forced removal.

If a baby tooth is knocked out prematurely due to injury, call Britt Moore Dental — early loss can affect spacing for permanent teeth. We'll advise you on whether intervention is needed.
item.excerpt:encoded: What to expect when your child starts losing baby teeth and when to consult a dentist.

item.title: What to Know If You Think You Have a Cracked Tooth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Cracked teeth are a surprisingly common problem. Symptoms include pain when biting, sensitivity to temperature, or a sharp pain that comes and goes. Cracks can be tricky to diagnose because they often don't show up on X-rays and may be invisible to the naked eye.

Treatment depends on the crack's severity — from bonding for minor cracks to crowns or root canals for deeper ones. Don't delay; an untreated crack can worsen. Call Britt Moore Dental for an evaluation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Cracked teeth are common and easily missed. Learn the symptoms and when to call your dentist.

item.title: Preventing Toddler Tooth Decay
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Toddler tooth decay is more common than many parents realize. The main culprits are sugary drinks in sippy cups, frequent snacking on sticky sweets, and inadequate brushing. Even 100% fruit juice contains significant sugar that feeds cavity-causing bacteria.

Limit juice to 4 oz per day, serve water between meals, brush twice daily with fluoride toothpaste, and schedule your toddler's dental checkup at Britt Moore Dental every six months.
item.excerpt:encoded: Practical tips to prevent cavity-causing tooth decay in toddlers and young children.

item.title: Why Adults Are Choosing Invisalign
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
More adults than ever are straightening their teeth — and many are choosing Invisalign over traditional braces. Clear aligners are virtually invisible, removable for eating and oral hygiene, and often more comfortable than metal brackets and wires. They're a practical choice for busy professionals.

Invisalign can treat many of the same issues as traditional braces. Ask at Britt Moore Dental whether Invisalign is right for your smile goals.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover why so many adults prefer Invisalign clear aligners over traditional metal braces.

item.title: Good Nutrition Leads to Healthy Mouths
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A nutritious diet is one of the most powerful ways to support your oral health. Calcium from dairy builds strong teeth. Vitamin C from citrus and vegetables supports gum health. Phosphorus from lean meats protects enamel. Water washes away food and bacteria while keeping tissues hydrated.

What you eat daily has a cumulative effect on your teeth and gums. Combine good nutrition with consistent oral hygiene and regular checkups at Britt Moore Dental for optimal oral health.
item.excerpt:encoded: Good nutrition directly supports oral health. Learn which nutrients benefit your teeth and gums.

item.title: Relax with Sedation Dentistry
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sedation dentistry allows patients who experience anxiety, low pain tolerance, or a strong gag reflex to receive dental treatment in a relaxed, comfortable state. Options include nitrous oxide (laughing gas), oral sedation, and IV sedation — each providing different levels of relaxation.

Many sedation patients are amazed at how easy their appointments become. Multiple procedures can often be completed in a single comfortable visit. Contact Britt Moore Dental to learn more about sedation options.
item.excerpt:encoded: Sedation dentistry makes dental visits comfortable and stress-free for anxious patients.

item.title: What Are the Benefits of Invisalign?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign clear aligners offer several advantages over traditional braces: they're nearly invisible, removable for meals and oral hygiene, generally more comfortable than brackets and wires, and typically require fewer office visits. Many patients achieve their desired results in 12–18 months.

Straighter teeth are also healthier teeth — properly aligned teeth are easier to clean and less prone to uneven wear. Contact Britt Moore Dental to find out if Invisalign is right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover the key benefits of Invisalign clear aligners for straightening your smile discreetly.

item.title: February Is Heart Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
February is American Heart Month, and there's a strong connection between heart health and oral health. Studies show that people with periodontal disease are significantly more likely to develop heart disease. The same bacteria responsible for gum disease may contribute to arterial inflammation.

Protecting your gums is protecting your heart. Schedule a periodontal evaluation at Britt Moore Dental this February as a gift to both your smile and your cardiovascular health.
item.excerpt:encoded: February is Heart Month — a reminder that gum health and heart health are deeply connected.

item.title: Heart Disease and Oral Health
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Multiple studies have established a link between periodontal disease and increased cardiovascular risk. Bacteria from infected gums can enter the bloodstream, travel to the heart, and contribute to arterial plaque buildup and inflammation associated with heart attacks and strokes.

The American Heart Association recognizes gum disease as an independent risk factor for heart disease. Regular dental cleanings and treatment of gum disease are important components of overall cardiovascular health management.
item.excerpt:encoded: The link between periodontal disease and heart disease is well-established. Learn what it means for you.

item.title: Dental Emergencies While Traveling
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental emergencies don't take vacations. If you experience a toothache, broken tooth, or lost crown while traveling, first contact your own dentist — we can often advise by phone. For severe pain, look for an emergency dental clinic or hospital in your location.

Pack a small dental emergency kit with dental cement (available at pharmacies), pain relievers, and dental wax. Before major trips, schedule a checkup at Britt Moore Dental to address any concerns in advance.
item.excerpt:encoded: Be prepared for dental emergencies while traveling with these tips and a basic dental kit.

item.title: Eating and Invisalign
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
One of Invisalign's biggest advantages is that aligners are removable for meals. Unlike braces, there are no dietary restrictions with Invisalign — you can eat whatever you like! Simply remove your aligners before eating, brush and floss, then put them back in. Aim to wear your aligners 20–22 hours per day for best results.

Avoid eating or drinking anything other than water while wearing aligners to prevent staining and damage. Ask Dr. Cimo for more Invisalign tips at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: With Invisalign, you can eat anything you like. Learn proper habits for meals during treatment.

item.title: Halloween Candy, Costumes, and More
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Halloween is all about fun — but the candy binge can be tough on teeth. The worst choices are sticky, chewy candies like caramel and gummies that cling to teeth for hours. Hard candies also pose a risk of chipping teeth. Better options include chocolate, which rinses away more easily.

Have a candy-sorting session with your kids, keep treat times to mealtimes rather than throughout the day, and make sure everyone brushes well before bed. Happy Halloween from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Halloween candy tips to protect your family's teeth while still enjoying the holiday tradition.

item.title: The Clear Benefits of Invisalign
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign has transformed orthodontic treatment for millions of patients. The clear aligner system is virtually invisible, removable, and comfortable — making it a popular choice for adults and teens who want a straighter smile without the look and feel of metal braces.

Invisalign uses a series of custom-made aligners that gradually move teeth into proper position. Results are predictable and precise. Ask about Invisalign at your next consultation at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign offers clear, comfortable, and removable orthodontic treatment. Learn more.

item.title: Are You a Candidate for Dental Implants?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Most adults with good general health and adequate jawbone density are good candidates for dental implants. Key factors include non-smoking or willingness to quit, no uncontrolled diabetes or bone disorders, and healthy gums. Even patients with some bone loss may qualify after bone grafting.

The best way to know is a consultation with Dr. Cimo. We'll take X-rays or a CT scan to evaluate your bone structure and determine if implants are the right solution for your missing teeth.
item.excerpt:encoded: Most healthy adults qualify for dental implants. Learn the key criteria for candidacy.

item.title: The Five Most Common Reasons for Emergency Visits
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The five most common dental emergencies we see are: severe toothache (often from an abscess), broken or chipped tooth from trauma, knocked-out tooth, lost filling or crown causing sensitivity, and soft tissue injury to the gums, lips, or tongue.

For any of these situations, call Britt Moore Dental immediately. We prioritize emergency patients and will do our best to see you the same day. Don't wait — prompt care prevents complications.
item.excerpt:encoded: Know the five most common dental emergencies and what to do when one happens to you.

item.title: The Invisalign Treatment Process
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The Invisalign process begins with a consultation and digital scan of your teeth. A series of custom clear aligners are fabricated, and you wear each set for 1–2 weeks before moving to the next. Office visits are typically every 6–8 weeks to monitor progress.

Most treatment plans take 12–18 months, though simple cases can be completed faster. Ask Dr. Cimo for a personalized Invisalign treatment timeline at your consultation at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: A step-by-step overview of the Invisalign treatment process from consultation to completion.

item.title: What Are Dental Implants?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental implants are titanium posts surgically placed into the jawbone to replace missing tooth roots. A crown is attached on top, creating a restoration that looks, feels, and functions like a natural tooth. Implants are considered the gold standard for single tooth replacement.

Unlike bridges, implants don't require altering neighboring teeth. They prevent bone loss and can last a lifetime with proper care. Schedule a dental implant consultation at Britt Moore Dental to explore your options.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental implants replace missing tooth roots and are the gold standard for tooth replacement.

item.title: How to Care for Your Invisalign Aligners
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Caring for your Invisalign aligners is straightforward. Rinse them every time you remove them, brush gently with a soft toothbrush and clear antibacterial soap, and soak them in Invisalign cleaning crystals or a diluted retainer cleaner daily. Always store them in the provided case when not in your mouth.

Never use toothpaste on your aligners — it's abrasive and can create tiny scratches where bacteria accumulate. Follow these tips for clear, odor-free aligners throughout your treatment at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Keep your Invisalign aligners clean and clear with these simple daily care habits.

item.title: Generic Clear Aligners vs. Invisalign
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Mail-order and generic clear aligner companies promise affordable straightening without dental supervision, but the risks are significant. Without proper examination, X-rays, and monitoring, underlying dental issues may be missed or worsened. Cases treated without professional oversight have resulted in root damage, bone loss, and relapse.

Invisalign, provided by a trained dental professional, includes comprehensive diagnosis and personalized treatment planning. Choose safety and proven results — ask about Invisalign at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Mail-order aligners carry real risks. Learn why professionally supervised Invisalign is the safer choice.

item.title: Summer Sports and Mouthguards
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer sports season brings increased risk of dental injuries. A properly fitted mouthguard is essential protective gear for contact sports, and even non-contact sports like cycling, skateboarding, and basketball pose impact risks.

Custom-fitted mouthguards from your dentist provide far superior protection compared to boil-and-bite options. They fit precisely, stay in place, allow easier breathing and speaking, and last longer. Ask about custom sports guards at Britt Moore Dental before your season starts.
item.excerpt:encoded: Custom sports mouthguards offer far better protection than store-bought alternatives for summer sports.

item.title: When Was Your Last Dental Checkup?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
If you can't remember your last dental checkup, it's definitely time to schedule one. Most dental problems develop gradually and painlessly until they become serious. Regular checkups catch cavities, gum disease, and oral cancer early — when they're easiest and least expensive to treat.

Don't wait for pain to motivate a visit. Make a commitment to your oral health by scheduling your next checkup at Britt Moore Dental today. Your smile will thank you.
item.excerpt:encoded: If you can't remember your last checkup, it's time to book one. Regular exams prevent costly problems.

item.title: The Best DH in Baseball
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
In baseball, the designated hitter (DH) steps in to do a specific job — just like your dental hygienist! Your hygienist is the MVP of your preventive care team, doing the specialized work of cleaning, scaling, and educating patients on home care.

Don't skip your hygiene appointments — they're your best defense against cavities and gum disease. Schedule your next cleaning at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: A fun baseball analogy celebrating the important role of your dental hygienist.

item.title: Make Every Day Earth Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Earth Day is a reminder to consider our environmental impact — including in our dental routines. Turn off the water while brushing, choose a bamboo toothbrush, use toothpaste tablets instead of plastic tubes, and recycle your dental floss container.

Small eco-friendly swaps add up to a big difference. Britt Moore Dental is committed to sustainable practices in our office as well. Happy Earth Day!
item.excerpt:encoded: Simple eco-friendly swaps to make your dental routine more sustainable every day.

item.title: Going Green for St. Patrick’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Happy St. Patrick's Day! Going green is great for the holiday, but keep in mind that green-colored treats and beverages can stain your teeth. Rinse with water after consuming colored foods and drinks to help minimize staining.

If your smile needs a refresh after the holiday fun, professional whitening at Britt Moore Dental can restore its brightness. Contact us to learn about our whitening options.
item.excerpt:encoded: St. Patrick's Day greetings with tips on protecting your smile from holiday staining.

item.title: The Transformation of Valentine’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Valentine's Day has evolved from a Roman festival into one of the biggest greeting card and candy holidays of the year. While we love celebrating love, we also love protecting smiles! Choose dark chocolate over sticky candies for a tooth-friendlier holiday treat.

Happy Valentine's Day from the team at Britt Moore Dental. Show your smile some love too — schedule your next cleaning today!
item.excerpt:encoded: A brief history of Valentine's Day and why dark chocolate is a smile-friendly choice.

item.title: Going Green for the New Year
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The new year is a perfect time to make your dental routine more eco-friendly. Switch to a bamboo toothbrush, choose toothpaste in recyclable packaging, use a water-efficient flosser, and turn off the faucet while brushing.

Going green doesn't mean sacrificing oral health. Britt Moore Dental encourages sustainable choices that are good for your smile and the planet. Happy New Year!
item.excerpt:encoded: Make your new year's dental routine more sustainable with these eco-friendly swaps.

item.title: New Year’s Day Around the World
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
New Year is celebrated with unique food traditions around the world — from lentils in Italy to grapes in Spain to black-eyed peas in the American South. Whatever your tradition, the new year is a great time to resolve to take better care of your smile.

Happy New Year from Britt Moore Dental! We wish you a healthy, happy year ahead — starting with a smile checkup in January.
item.excerpt:encoded: New Year's greetings and a reminder to start the year with a dental health resolution.

item.title: Happy Holidays, Healthy Holidays
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The holiday season brings wonderful traditions — and plenty of sweet treats. Enjoy holiday foods mindfully: sip water between sugary drinks, limit sticky candies and hard candies that can crack teeth, and maintain your brushing and flossing routine even when your schedule gets hectic.

Happy Holidays from the entire team at Britt Moore Dental! We look forward to seeing your smile in the new year.
item.excerpt:encoded: Holiday greetings and tips to keep your smile healthy during the festive season.

item.title: Thanksgiving in North America
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Thanksgiving is a time for gratitude, family, and abundant food. We're grateful for the trust our patients place in us! The Thanksgiving feast actually contains many tooth-friendly foods — turkey, sweet potatoes, and cranberries all have dental health benefits.

Enjoy your holiday meal and remember to brush and floss before bed. Happy Thanksgiving from all of us at Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Thanksgiving greetings and the tooth-friendly foods you can enjoy guilt-free at the feast.

item.title: Germs Living on My Toothbrush? Say It Ain’t So!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Yes, your toothbrush can harbor bacteria from your mouth and environment — but don't panic. Rinsing thoroughly after each use, storing it upright to air dry, and replacing it every 3–4 months (or after illness) keeps bacteria levels low. Never store wet toothbrushes in a closed container.

The benefits of brushing far outweigh any germ concern. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental about proper toothbrush hygiene at your next visit.
item.excerpt:encoded: Your toothbrush can harbor bacteria — but proper care keeps it clean and effective.

item.title: Take Your Pick
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to interdental cleaning, you have options beyond traditional floss. Floss picks, interdental brushes, and water flossers all effectively clean between teeth when used correctly. The best choice depends on your tooth spacing, dexterity, and personal preference.

The most important thing is that you clean between your teeth every day by some method. Ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental which interdental cleaning tool is right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Compare floss, picks, interdental brushes, and water flossers to find your best interdental cleaner.

item.title: October Is National Dental Hygiene Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
October is National Dental Hygiene Month — a perfect time to recommit to your oral care routine. A simple, effective routine doesn't have to be complicated: brush for two minutes after breakfast and before bed, floss once daily, and use a fluoride rinse. That's it!

Even the busiest schedule has four minutes for brushing. Make dental hygiene a non-negotiable part of your day, and thank your dental hygienist at Britt Moore Dental with a great checkup.
item.excerpt:encoded: October is National Dental Hygiene Month. Recommit to a simple, effective daily routine.

item.title: Summer Smiles
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer is the season of outdoor adventures, beach days, and big smiles for photos. It's also a great time to schedule dental treatments you may have been putting off — many families find summer easier to coordinate appointments.

Schedule the whole family for checkups, address any cosmetic concerns before summer events, and keep smiling! Contact Britt Moore Dental to book your summer appointments today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Summer is the perfect time to schedule dental checkups and cosmetic treatments for the whole family.

item.title: Celebrate Labor Day by Getting Away
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Labor Day weekend means a well-deserved rest and possibly a quick getaway. Before you pack, make sure your travel dental kit includes a toothbrush, toothpaste, floss, and any dental appliances you wear. Schedule a pre-travel checkup to make sure no dental problems are waiting to ruin your trip.

Happy Labor Day from Britt Moore Dental! Enjoy the long weekend, and we'll see you when you return.
item.excerpt:encoded: Labor Day greetings and tips for keeping your dental routine intact while traveling.

item.title: Whitening an Artificial Tooth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Unlike natural teeth, dental restorations such as crowns, veneers, bonding, and implant crowns cannot be whitened with bleaching agents. If your natural teeth are whitened but your restorations stay the same shade, a color mismatch may result.

The solution is to whiten your natural teeth first, then replace any visible restorations to match the new shade. Discuss whitening plans that account for your restorations with Dr. Cimo at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental restorations can't be whitened. Here's how to plan whitening around crowns and veneers.

item.title: Sippy Cups
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sippy cups are a practical transition from bottle to regular cup, but they can harm teeth if misused. Filling them with juice, milk, or sweet beverages — especially for comfort or sleep — bathes teeth in sugar for extended periods, promoting tooth decay.

Fill sippy cups with water between meals. Milk and juice should be offered at mealtimes only and in limited quantities. Ask Dr. Cimo about healthy toddler beverage habits at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: How to use sippy cups without harming your toddler's developing teeth.

item.title: The Dog Days of Summer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The hottest days of summer call for extra hydration — and your teeth need it too. Dehydration reduces saliva flow, which increases cavity and bad breath risk. Make water your go-to drink all day, every day.

Skip the sugary sports drinks and sodas. Pure water is the best thing you can drink for your teeth and your body. Stay cool, stay hydrated, and keep smiling this summer with Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Stay hydrated this summer for the sake of your smile — water is always the best choice.

item.title: Why Do Molars Seem to Get More Cavities?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Molars are cavity magnets because of their deep grooves and pits where food and bacteria easily accumulate. They're also harder to reach with a toothbrush, making thorough cleaning more challenging. Children's molars are especially vulnerable as they develop.

Dental sealants are highly effective at protecting molar grooves from decay. Ask Dr. Cimo about sealants for your children at Britt Moore Dental — they're a simple, painless investment in cavity prevention.
item.excerpt:encoded: Deep grooves make molars cavity-prone. Dental sealants offer effective protection.

item.title: Adults Can Get Cavities Too
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Many adults assume cavities are a childhood problem, but that's a dangerous misconception. Adults face cavity risks from root exposure due to gum recession, dry mouth from medications, and decades of wear. Root cavities in particular are common in adults over 50.

Regular checkups and maintaining excellent home care throughout your life are essential. Don't assume you've outgrown cavities — schedule your exam at Britt Moore Dental today.
item.excerpt:encoded: Cavities aren't just for kids. Learn why adult cavity risk increases with age.

item.title: Water Features
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Water is the single best beverage for your oral health. It rinses away food particles, neutralizes acid, keeps your saliva production up, and — when fluoridated — helps strengthen enamel. Drinking water throughout the day is one of the simplest and most effective things you can do for your teeth.

Make water your default drink and reach for it instead of soda, juice, or sports drinks. Your teeth will thank you at every checkup at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Water is the best drink for your dental health. Learn all the ways it benefits your smile.

item.title: Your Options for Sedation Dentistry
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sedation dentistry offers multiple levels of relaxation to suit different needs. Nitrous oxide (laughing gas) provides mild relaxation while you remain fully awake. Oral conscious sedation induces a deeper but still conscious relaxed state. IV sedation provides the deepest relaxation available in a dental office.

All sedation options at Britt Moore Dental are administered by qualified professionals in a safe, monitored environment. Contact us to discuss which sedation level is appropriate for your needs.
item.excerpt:encoded: Compare sedation dentistry options from nitrous oxide to IV sedation to find your best fit.

item.title: Celebrate Your Mom’s Smile with These Mother’s Day Gifts
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Give Mom the gift of a great smile this Mother's Day! An electric toothbrush, a professional whitening kit, or a dental spa package makes a thoughtful and practical gift that shows you care about her health and confidence.

A gift card toward cosmetic dental treatment is also a wonderful choice for the mom who has everything. Happy Mother's Day from Britt Moore Dental — celebrate the beautiful smiles in your life!
item.excerpt:encoded: Thoughtful dental-related Mother's Day gift ideas that celebrate her smile and health.

item.title: Gum Disease in Children
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Gum disease isn't just an adult problem — children can develop gingivitis too, especially during puberty when hormonal changes increase gum sensitivity. Signs include red, swollen, or bleeding gums. Left untreated, childhood gingivitis can progress and affect permanent teeth.

Teaching proper brushing and flossing early, combined with regular dental visits, is the best prevention. Make sure your child's next checkup at Britt Moore Dental includes a periodontal evaluation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Children can develop gum disease too. Learn the signs and how to prevent it in kids.

item.title: Treating Gum Disease with Antibiotics
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
In some cases, antibiotics are used alongside scaling and root planing to treat advanced gum disease. Locally delivered antibiotics like Arestin can be placed directly into periodontal pockets to target bacteria at the source. Systemic antibiotics may also be prescribed for severe infections.

Antibiotic therapy is not a standalone treatment — it works best as a complement to thorough mechanical cleaning. Ask Dr. Cimo whether antibiotic therapy is appropriate for your gum disease at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: How antibiotics can complement deep cleaning to treat advanced periodontal disease.

item.title: Dreaming of a Stunning Smile Without Using Braces
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Want a straighter, more beautiful smile without traditional braces? Several options exist beyond metal brackets: clear aligners like Invisalign move teeth gradually with removable trays. Porcelain veneers can create the appearance of perfectly aligned teeth by masking minor alignment issues and gaps.

Schedule a smile makeover consultation at Britt Moore Dental to explore which approach best suits your goals, timeline, and budget.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign and veneers offer paths to a stunning smile without traditional metal braces.

item.title: How to Prevent Dry Socket
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dry socket (alveolar osteitis) occurs when the blood clot in a tooth extraction site is dislodged before healing is complete. It's painful and delays healing. Prevention is straightforward: avoid smoking, using straws, spitting forcefully, or eating hard foods in the days following extraction.

Follow all post-extraction instructions provided by Britt Moore Dental carefully. If you develop severe pain 2–4 days after an extraction, call us immediately — dry socket is treatable.
item.excerpt:encoded: What dry socket is, how to prevent it after tooth extraction, and how it's treated.

item.title: Dental Insurance Benefits
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental insurance is designed to help you afford preventive care and reduce the financial burden of necessary treatments. Most plans cover preventive care (cleanings, exams, X-rays) at 100%, basic restorative care at 80%, and major restorative care at 50%. Annual maximums typically range from $1,000–$2,000.

Use your benefits wisely by prioritizing preventive care. The team at Britt Moore Dental will help you understand your coverage and maximize your benefits. Call us to verify your insurance before your appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Understand how dental insurance works and how to maximize your annual benefits.

item.title: Tooth Protection and Winter Sports
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Winter sports like skiing, hockey, and snowboarding carry dental injury risks. A custom-fitted mouthguard provides essential protection against impact injuries. Even cold air can trigger sensitivity in patients with thin enamel or gum recession — breathing through your nose helps.

Don't hit the slopes without protection. Ask about custom sports mouthguards at Britt Moore Dental before your winter sports season begins.
item.excerpt:encoded: Winter sports pose dental risks. Learn about protection with custom mouthguards and cold sensitivity.

item.title: A Brighter Smile for the New Year
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
What better way to start the new year than with a brighter, more confident smile? Professional teeth whitening is one of the fastest and most affordable cosmetic dental improvements available. Results last a year or more with proper care.

Make a smile resolution this new year and stick to it. Schedule your whitening consultation at Britt Moore Dental and enter the new year glowing with confidence.
item.excerpt:encoded: Start the new year with a brighter smile through professional teeth whitening.

item.title: Are Thumb Sucking and Pacifier Habits Harmful for a Child’s Teeth?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Thumb sucking and pacifier use are normal in infants and provide comfort and security. However, prolonged habits beyond age 3–4 can cause bite problems, jaw misalignment, and speech issues as permanent teeth develop. Pacifiers are generally easier to wean than thumb sucking.

If your child is still using a pacifier or thumb sucking past age 3, discuss strategies with Dr. Cimo at Britt Moore Dental at your next visit.
item.excerpt:encoded: Thumb sucking is normal in infants but can cause problems if it persists beyond age 3–4.

item.title: The Perks of Dairy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dairy products are among the best foods for your dental health. Cheese, yogurt, and milk are rich in calcium and phosphorus that strengthen enamel. Cheese also increases saliva production and contains casein protein that forms a protective layer on teeth. Hard cheeses can actually help clean tooth surfaces.

Add dairy to your diet for stronger teeth — and ask your hygienist at Britt Moore Dental for more dietary tips to support your oral health.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dairy products strengthen enamel and protect teeth. Learn the dental benefits of cheese and yogurt.

item.title: When Should a Filling Be Replaced?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Fillings don't last forever. Signs that a filling may need replacement include visible cracks or chips, pain when biting, sensitivity to temperature, dark spots around the filling margins, or the feeling that something has changed with your bite. Composite fillings typically last 7–10 years; amalgam can last longer.

Your dentist monitors all existing restorations at checkups. Don't wait for pain to signal a problem — schedule your exam at Britt Moore Dental regularly.
item.excerpt:encoded: Know the signs that indicate a dental filling needs to be replaced before it causes problems.

item.title: The Hazards of Smokeless Tobacco
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Smokeless tobacco (chewing tobacco, snuff, dip) is not a safe alternative to smoking. It causes severe gum recession, tooth abrasion, tooth loss, and significantly increases the risk of oral cancer. Users who start young face decades of accumulated damage and addiction.

If you use smokeless tobacco, regular oral cancer screenings are critically important. Britt Moore Dental provides comprehensive screenings and can help connect you with cessation resources. Your oral health depends on quitting.
item.excerpt:encoded: Smokeless tobacco is not safe — it causes gum disease and dramatically increases oral cancer risk.

item.title: More Adults Are Opting for Invisalign
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Adult orthodontic treatment has never been more popular, and Invisalign is leading the way. Clear aligner technology allows professionals, parents, and retirees alike to straighten their smiles discreetly without the social stigma of metal braces. Millions of adults have already achieved beautiful results with Invisalign.

It's never too late to love your smile. Schedule an Invisalign consultation at Britt Moore Dental to see how clear aligners can transform your teeth.
item.excerpt:encoded: Adult Invisalign use is booming. Learn why clear aligners are the discreet choice for grown-ups.

item.title: Chipped Your Tooth – Now What?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A chipped tooth can range from minor cosmetic issue to dental emergency depending on its severity. A small chip on a back tooth may wait a day or two; a chip exposing nerve tissue or causing significant sensitivity needs same-day attention. Cover any sharp edges with dental wax to protect your tongue and cheek.

Treatment options include bonding (for minor chips), veneers, or crowns for more significant damage. Call Britt Moore Dental to determine the urgency and best treatment approach.
item.excerpt:encoded: What to do immediately when you chip a tooth and what treatment options are available.

item.title: Are There Foods That Whiten Teeth?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
While no food can bleach your teeth like professional whitening, some foods naturally help maintain a brighter smile. Crunchy fruits and vegetables like apples, celery, and carrots act as natural scrubbers. Strawberries contain malic acid that may lighten surface stains slightly. Dairy products reduce staining by depositing minerals on enamel.

These foods support a clean smile but cannot substitute for professional whitening. For significant brightening, ask about whitening options at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Some foods naturally help maintain a brighter smile, though they can't replace professional whitening.

item.title: Alleviate Tooth Sensitivity
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth sensitivity can range from mild discomfort to sharp, debilitating pain. Steps to reduce sensitivity include using a desensitizing toothpaste with potassium nitrate or stannous fluoride, switching to a soft toothbrush, avoiding acidic foods and drinks, and wearing a night guard if you grind.

Professional treatments including fluoride varnish, dental bonding, and gum grafts can provide more lasting relief. Don't just tolerate sensitivity — get it diagnosed and treated at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Practical steps and professional treatments to alleviate chronic tooth sensitivity.

item.title: Caring for Your Smile After Invisalign Treatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Completing Invisalign treatment is exciting, but the work isn't quite done. Teeth naturally tend to shift back toward their original positions after orthodontic treatment. Wearing a retainer as instructed is essential to maintaining your results long-term.

Most patients wear retainers nightly indefinitely to preserve their smile. Ask Dr. Cimo about retainer options at Britt Moore Dental and make sure your beautiful new smile lasts a lifetime.
item.excerpt:encoded: Post-Invisalign retainer wear is essential for maintaining your straightened smile long-term.

item.title: Caring for Your Smile After Invisalign Treatment (2)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
After Invisalign treatment, consistent retainer use is the key to a lasting, beautiful smile. In addition to wearing your retainer, maintain excellent oral hygiene — straight teeth are easier to clean and less prone to cavities and gum disease.

Schedule a post-treatment checkup at Britt Moore Dental to confirm your teeth have settled into their final positions and get fitted for your permanent retainer if needed.
item.excerpt:encoded: Maintain your Invisalign results long-term with proper retainer use and continued oral care.

item.title: Invisalign and Invisalign Teen (2)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign Teen is specially designed for younger patients with features like compliance indicators (blue dots that fade with proper wear) and replacement aligners for those that get lost. Teens benefit from the same discreet, comfortable treatment as adults while keeping up with sports and social activities.

Ask about Invisalign Teen at Britt Moore Dental for a treatment option that fits your teenager's busy lifestyle.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign Teen offers the same discreet straightening with features designed for younger patients.

item.title: Valentine’s Day History
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Valentine's Day is named after Saint Valentine, a Roman priest who reportedly performed secret marriages for soldiers. Today it's celebrated with flowers, candy, and heartfelt cards. From a dental perspective, chocolate is a better choice than sticky sweets — it rinses off teeth more easily.

Celebrate love this Valentine's Day, and celebrate your smile by scheduling your next cleaning at Britt Moore Dental. We love your smile!
item.excerpt:encoded: The history of Valentine's Day and why chocolate is the smile-friendly holiday treat choice.

item.title: Caring for Your Smile While Wearing Invisalign
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Good oral hygiene during Invisalign treatment is easier than with braces because aligners are removable. Remove aligners before eating or drinking anything other than water. Brush and floss after meals before reinserting aligners to prevent trapping food and bacteria.

Clean your aligners daily and bring them to every appointment at Britt Moore Dental for evaluation. Proper hygiene during treatment ensures your teeth emerge healthy and beautiful.
item.excerpt:encoded: How to maintain excellent oral hygiene and clean teeth during your Invisalign treatment.

item.title: Is Invisalign Right for You?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign treats a wide range of alignment issues including crowding, spacing, overbite, underbite, and crossbite. Ideal candidates are adults and teens with mild to moderate alignment concerns who are committed to wearing aligners 20–22 hours daily. Severe cases may require traditional braces.

The best way to know if Invisalign is right for you is a consultation at Britt Moore Dental. We'll evaluate your teeth and discuss your goals to create a personalized treatment plan.
item.excerpt:encoded: Find out if Invisalign can treat your specific alignment issues and what to expect from treatment.

item.title: Thanksgiving Trivia
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Did you know the traditional Thanksgiving feast features many foods that are actually good for your teeth? Turkey is high in protein and phosphorus that support enamel. Sweet potatoes provide vitamins A and C for gum health. Even cranberry sauce has oral health benefits with its antioxidants and anti-adhesive properties against bacteria.

Happy Thanksgiving from all of us at Britt Moore Dental! Enjoy the feast and remember to brush before bed.
item.excerpt:encoded: Fun Thanksgiving dental trivia — many traditional feast foods are actually good for your teeth!

item.title: Cold Comfort
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Winter cold and dry air can worsen existing tooth sensitivity. Cold air contacting exposed dentin can trigger sharp pain. Breathing through your nose when possible helps, as does using a sensitive toothpaste and ensuring any underlying causes of recession or wear are addressed.

If cold weather regularly triggers tooth pain, that sensitivity deserves professional evaluation. Schedule an appointment at Britt Moore Dental and get to the root of the problem.
item.excerpt:encoded: Cold weather can worsen tooth sensitivity. Learn why and how to find lasting relief.

item.title: Dental X-Rays and Your Child
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental X-rays are safe and important for children. They allow the dentist to spot cavities between teeth that aren't visible during an exam, monitor developing permanent teeth, and detect any orthodontic concerns early. Digital X-rays use a very small amount of radiation — far less than a day of natural background radiation.

Children typically need X-rays every 6–12 months depending on their cavity risk. Lead aprons protect their growing bodies during every X-ray at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Dental X-rays are safe and essential for monitoring your child's developing teeth and detecting cavities.

item.title: Beneficial Beverage or Damaging Drink?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Not all beverages are equal when it comes to your dental health. Water is the gold standard — hydrating and non-damaging. Milk supports enamel with calcium. Green tea has antibacterial properties. On the harmful side: sodas, sports drinks, citrus juices, and wine are all acidic and erosive with regular consumption.

Make informed beverage choices and rinse with water after acidic drinks. Ask your dental team at Britt Moore Dental for personalized guidance at your next visit.
item.excerpt:encoded: A guide to which beverages are beneficial or harmful for your teeth and why.

item.title: Some Benefits to Giving Your Smile an Extra Boost
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Cosmetic dental improvements go beyond aesthetics — they can have a meaningful impact on your confidence, social interactions, and even career success. Studies show that people with attractive smiles are often perceived as more successful and approachable.

Whether it's whitening, veneers, straightening, or a complete smile makeover, investing in your smile is investing in yourself. Schedule a cosmetic consultation at Britt Moore Dental to discover your smile's potential.
item.excerpt:encoded: Cosmetic dental improvements boost confidence and can positively impact your social and professional life.

item.title: Invisalign and Invisalign Teen Make You Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign and Invisalign Teen have helped millions of patients achieve beautifully straight smiles without the hassle of metal braces. The virtually invisible aligners allow patients to smile confidently throughout treatment — not just after it.

From initial consultation to final retainer fitting, the Britt Moore Dental team guides you through every step of Invisalign treatment. Start your smile journey today — call us for a consultation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign lets you smile confidently throughout treatment, not just after. Learn how it works.

item.title: Hot Day – Three Drinks to Leave Home When You’re Packing the Cooler
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When packing for a hot day outdoors, consider leaving these three drinks at home: energy drinks (highly acidic and full of sugar), flavored sparkling water (more acidic than plain water), and citrus juice (very acidic, especially concentrated). Instead, pack plenty of plain water and milk for kids.

Keep your family's smiles protected this summer. Water is always the best choice. Britt Moore Dental wishes you a fun, smile-healthy summer!
item.excerpt:encoded: Three common summer drinks that are surprisingly damaging to your tooth enamel.

item.title: Happy Fourth of July
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Happy Fourth of July from all of us at Britt Moore Dental! As you celebrate with fireworks, BBQs, and family gatherings, enjoy the feast while being mindful of tooth-unfriendly treats like hard candies, ice, and sticky caramel-coated foods.

Choose water over sugary drinks, watch out for popcorn kernels, and remember to brush and floss before the evening's fireworks show. Have a safe, happy, and smile-healthy Independence Day!
item.excerpt:encoded: Happy Fourth of July greetings with tips for keeping your smile healthy during the holiday celebrations.

item.title: Is a Lost Tooth a Lost Cause?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A knocked-out permanent tooth is absolutely not a lost cause — if you act quickly. Pick up the tooth by the crown (not the root), gently rinse without scrubbing, and try to reinsert it into the socket. If reinsertion isn't possible, store it in milk or saliva and call your dentist immediately. A tooth reimplanted within 30–60 minutes has the best chance of survival.

Call Britt Moore Dental the moment a tooth is knocked out. Time is critical. We do everything possible to save your natural tooth.
item.excerpt:encoded: A knocked-out tooth can be saved if you act quickly. Learn the critical steps to take immediately.

item.title: Happy Labor Day!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Labor Day is celebrated on the first Monday of September each year. It is a federal holiday where Americans honor their work achievements and contributions to the nation’s prosperity and well-being.

Canada was the first country to celebrate Labor Day, with the US following suit. President Cleveland officially established Labor Day as a US holiday in 1894. Over 30 million Americans travel during Labor Day weekend annually, and Americans consume approximately seven billion hot dogs by the end of Labor Day.

We hope you enjoy the final days of summer — barbequing, visiting the lake, and spending time with family. From all of us at Britt Moore Dental, have a safe and happy Labor Day weekend!
item.excerpt:encoded: Labor Day is a federal holiday where Americans honor their work achievements. Happy Labor Day from Britt Moore Dental!

item.title: Fall’s in the Air? Think Fall Dental Care
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Whether you already miss the sun’s bright rays, or can’t wait for some cool, crisp weather and colorful leaves, summer is making way for fall. The change of seasons might mean it’s time for some adjustments to your dental care routine.

Fall Tooth Sensitivity

You might enjoy the brisk weather, but sensitivity can be the sign of a cracked tooth, gum disease, or even too-energetic brushing. If you’re experiencing sensitivity with hot and cold foods, give us a call and we’ll get to the root of your problem.

Fall Sports

A mouth guard is essential for autumn contact sports like football or soccer, and is also a good idea for biking and skateboarding where a fall or collision is possible.

Fall Feasts

‘Tis the season for sugary Halloween treats, Thanksgiving desserts, and holiday lattes. Celebrate the season and your dental health by enjoying these treats in moderation. Autumn’s harvest also brings nutritious offerings like Brussels sprouts, sweet potatoes, carrots, and apples, rich in nutrients that help keep teeth and gums healthy.

Don’t forget to call our Houston, Texas office for your regular checkup. Spring, summer, winter, fall — it’s always the right season for taking care of your dental health!
item.excerpt:encoded: As summer gives way to fall, it's time to adjust your dental routine. From sensitivity to fall sports mouthguards, we've got you covered.

item.title: Invisalign® Q&A
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
It’s estimated that up to four million people at any one time are wearing braces in the United States and Canada. Braces have come a long way, and today, options are available that are virtually invisible. The most popular of these is Invisalign.

Are they really invisible? Yes, the clear liners are virtually invisible, enabling patients to feel confident about their appearance while receiving orthodontic treatment.

Are there wires, rubber bands, and brackets? No. Invisalign uses clear liners that can be easily removed and cleaned throughout the day.

Food Restrictions? No. Aligners are simply removed when eating, then replaced after brushing away leftover food particles.

Cost Comparison: Invisalign costs about the same as traditional braces, making it an affordable alternative.

Oral Hygiene: Invisalign can be removed at any time for regular brushing and flossing without special tools. Contact our Houston office to schedule a consultation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Have questions about Invisalign? We answer the most common questions about clear aligner treatment.

item.title: Thanks to Invisalign®, You Can Have a Perfect Smile Without Metal Wires or Brackets!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to straightening your smile, having a mouth full of metal braces may not be your idea of fun. Luckily, we can offer a cosmetic alternative: Invisalign!

Using a series of clear, comfortable, and completely customized aligners, you can get the smile you’ve always wanted without traditional braces. Since Invisalign aligners are discreet, they’re hardly noticeable when you speak and smile. Because they’re removable, you can eat and drink whatever you want — just remember to brush and floss before putting your aligners back in! Treatment with Invisalign is just as effective as with metal braces, and the results are just as amazing.

If you want to know more about Invisalign, contact our Houston, Texas office to schedule an appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign lets you straighten your smile with clear, comfortable, removable aligners — no metal wires or brackets required.

item.title: ‘Tis the Season—for Healthy Dental Choices!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The holiday season brings many tempting treats, but some are harder on your teeth than others. Candy canes, peppermints, and other hard candies are potentially bad for your teeth when you crunch away, and definitely bad if you let them dissolve slowly. Gumdrops and chewy toffees stick to fillings, crowns, and orthodontic hardware.

The good news: choose softer alternatives like dark chocolate, enjoy sweets during meals to boost saliva production, rinse with water afterward, and brush — though wait 30 minutes after acidic foods.

Maintaining good oral hygiene throughout the holiday season will help you start the new year with a healthy smile. From our team at Britt Moore Dental, happy holidays!
item.excerpt:encoded: The holiday season brings sweet temptations. Here's how to enjoy treats while keeping your smile healthy.

item.title: It’s a Wrap: Ending the Year with a Smile!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Pope Gregory XIII declared January 1st to be the day on which everyone would celebrate the New Year back in 1582, and the tradition of ringing in the new year with celebration has continued ever since.

As you prepare for New Year’s Eve, our team has three suggestions: Be safe — if you’re celebrating with alcohol, plan ahead with a designated driver or overnight accommodations. Enjoy family and friends — coordinate schedules and choose activities everyone enjoys. And accessorize with a smile — a beautiful, healthy smile is one of the best accessories you can add to any attire.

Happy New Year from Britt Moore Dental! Here’s to a full year of opportunity ahead.
item.excerpt:encoded: As the year ends, here are three tips for a safe and happy New Year's Eve celebration.

item.title: Make This the Year You Stop Smoking
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A new year offers an opportunity to quit smoking and improve your overall health. Smoking damages the body through increased risks of lung disease, heart attacks, strokes, cancer, and blood vessel disorders — and the effects on oral health are equally severe.

Tar and nicotine stain teeth right away. Smokers also experience greater buildup of plaque and tartar, increased bad breath, and significantly higher risk of gum disease. Smoking is linked to higher rates of dental implant failure, slower healing after oral procedures, and increased risk of dry socket after extractions.

Most seriously, smoking is the single most serious risk factor for oral cancer — though quitting significantly reduces this risk. Consult your healthcare provider for cessation strategies, and call our Houston office to discuss how quitting can improve your oral health.
item.excerpt:encoded: Smoking harms your oral health in many ways — from staining and gum disease to oral cancer. Make this the year you quit.

item.title: Easing Your Allergies with Latex-Free Dentistry
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A latex allergy represents a hypersensitivity to latex proteins. The CDC recommends that individuals with this allergy avoid direct contact with any materials that contain latex. While latex gloves are the primary culprit, other dental materials can trigger reactions ranging from mild symptoms like runny nose and itchy eyes to more serious reactions including asthma and cardiovascular problems.

Creating a Latex-Safe Dental Environment

A dental practice committed to latex safety screens all patients for allergies, eliminates latex gloves for all personnel, removes latex products from treatment areas including rubber dams and elastics, and keeps work surfaces clean of latex powder.

For patients with documented latex allergies, finding a dentist offering a latex-free environment is essential. Contact our Houston office to discuss latex-free dentistry options or to schedule an appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Latex allergies can complicate dental visits. Learn how our latex-free practice keeps allergy-sensitive patients safe and comfortable.

item.title: What’s the Advantage to Invisalign®?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign is an excellent alternative to traditional metal braces. During your initial consultation, our team takes an impression of your teeth and uses specialized software to analyze your current tooth positioning and plan your ideal alignment.

Invisalign treatment consists of a series of aligners that you switch out approximately every two weeks, gradually shifting your teeth into proper position. The aligners can address crowded teeth, crossbites, overbites, and underbites.

Key Advantages: Clear appearance — aligners are virtually invisible. Removable design allows you to take them out during meals and teeth cleaning. Schedule a consultation at our Houston, Texas location to determine if you’re a candidate for Invisalign treatment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign offers clear, removable aligners that straighten your teeth discreetly. Learn about the treatment process and advantages.

item.title: Who Benefits from Invisalign®?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The simple answer to who benefits from Invisalign is “almost everyone.” The transparent aligners are custom-molded for each patient’s mouth, removable during meals and cleaning, and apply gentle force to teeth.

Benefits to Adults: Invisalign straightens your teeth with virtually invisible aligners. You can remove them while eating, avoiding dietary restrictions. For special occasions, temporary removal causes no damage to your treatment progress.

Benefits to Teenagers: Athletes can wear standard mouthguards, and musicians avoid metal interfering with instrument playing. Oral hygiene also improves since brushing and flossing are simpler without navigating wires and brackets.

For consultations regarding Invisalign or Invisalign Teen, contact our Houston, Texas office.
item.excerpt:encoded: From adults wanting a discreet option to teens in sports, Invisalign benefits nearly everyone who wants a straighter smile.

item.title: Go Green for St. Patrick’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
St. Patrick’s Day is approaching, and there’s more than one way to go green! Beyond wearing a simple green shirt, here are some creative ways to celebrate the holiday.

Visit Chicago’s Green River: Chicago has been celebrating the holiday with this tradition for more than 50 years, with tens of thousands gathering to witness the famous river-dyeing ceremony.

Don Green Face Paint: Similar to sports fans on game day, use face paint designed for skin application to showcase your holiday spirit.

Eat Green All Day: Make green smoothies using leafy vegetables like spinach or kale blended with fruit, ice, milk, or juice. Extend the theme throughout the day with green soups and egg salads while gaining nutritional benefits.

Happy St. Patrick’s Day from our team at Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Beyond wearing green, there are creative ways to celebrate St. Patrick's Day this year. Here are our team's favorite ideas.

item.title: Mouthguard Q&A
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A mouthguard is a soft plastic, flexible removable device fitted to your upper teeth. It protects your teeth, jaws, lips, tongue, cheeks, and gums during contact sports and recreational activities.

How do mouthguards work? Mouthguards function as shock absorbers, cushioning blows to the face, head, or neck. They prevent tooth fractures and lacerations to soft tissues while potentially reducing concussion and TMJ dislocation risks.

When should you wear one? Wear a mouthguard during activities involving fall or head contact risks, including football, baseball, basketball, soccer, and hockey.

How to choose a mouthguard? Options include preformed boil-to-fit models from sports stores, but we recommend custom-fabricated mouthguards for superior comfort and injury prevention. Contact our office for personalized recommendations.
item.excerpt:encoded: Everything you need to know about mouthguards — how they work, when to wear them, and which type is best for you.

item.title: Oral Cancer Awareness Month
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
April is Oral Cancer Awareness Month. Oral cancer can occur anywhere in the mouth, including the tongue, lips, gums, inside the cheek, or the roof or floor of the mouth. Every year, more than 8,000 people die from oral cancer.

The reason oral cancer has a higher death rate than many other cancers is that it often goes undetected until it has become too advanced and spread to another part of the body.

Risk Factors Include:

  • Age: Most patients who develop oral cancer are above the age of 40.
  • Tobacco: Excessive tobacco use is a substantial contributor.
  • Alcohol: Excessive consumption damages DNA and blocks cellular repair, especially when combined with tobacco.
  • Sun Exposure: Your lips need SPF too — repeated sun exposure increases the risk of lip cancer.

Regular dental visits are the first line of defense against oral cancer. Schedule a screening with our Houston office today.
item.excerpt:encoded: April is Oral Cancer Awareness Month. Learn about risk factors and why regular dental screenings are so important for early detection.

item.title: Every Day is Earth Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Meaningful environmental action doesn’t require dramatic gestures. The little things you do can add up to make a great impact in your community.

Textile Recycling: According to the EPA, nearly 21 million tons of textiles enter American landfills annually. Donating unwanted clothing to secondhand retailers reduces solid waste.

Reducing Disposables: Replacing single-use items like plastic water bottles with reusable alternatives significantly decreases environmental harm.

Water Conservation: Did you know that if all Americans turned off the water while brushing their teeth, more than 1.5 million gallons of water could be conserved daily?

Energy Efficiency: Turning off lights when leaving a room for 15 or more minutes conserves energy and reduces cooling costs.

Happy Earth Day from Dr. Philip J. Cimo and the Britt Moore Dental team!
item.excerpt:encoded: Small everyday actions add up to meaningful environmental impact. Here are four simple ways to celebrate Earth Day all year long.

item.title: Tips for Using Invisalign®
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Here are key tips for optimizing your Invisalign treatment:

Teeth Whitening: Always ask us about teeth whitening timing. Attachments may not whiten properly, so it’s worth discussing the best approach with our dental team.

Maintain Flossing: Despite wearing aligners, continue daily flossing. Bacteria can accumulate behind the aligners, potentially compromising dental and gum health.

Follow the 48-Hour Rule: When you insert every new set of aligners, leave them in as much as possible during the first 48 hours for maximum tooth movement and effectiveness.

Managing Discomfort: Slight discomfort may occur and over-the-counter pain relievers can help. If you experience significant pain, contact our Houston office promptly.
item.excerpt:encoded: Get the most out of your Invisalign treatment with these helpful tips from our dental team.

item.title: Wishing All Our Moms a Happy Mother’s Day!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
“Motherhood: All love begins and ends there.” — Robert Browning

To all the mothers who support their children’s oral health — driving kids to routine appointments, providing comfort during treatment, and making sure the toothbrush gets used twice a day — we are grateful for you. Both Dr. Cimo and our entire staff appreciate the mothers who visit Britt Moore Dental with their families.

Happy Mother’s Day! We hope you enjoy your special day with those who love you most.
item.excerpt:encoded: To all the moms who support their children's dental health — Happy Mother's Day from Britt Moore Dental!

item.title: Make Your Child’s Next Visit to Our Office Great!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Preparing young children for dental visits can make all the difference. Here are our best tips for a smooth, positive appointment.

Before the Appointment: Use simple explanations like describing the dentist as “a doctor who helps keep your teeth strong and healthy.” Read children’s books about dental visits and use playtime to count teeth or play pretend dentist with toys.

During the Visit: Your attitude is contagious! Arriving early allows children to acclimate to the environment. Bring a favorite toy for comfort. Our team is experienced with children who may initially be hesitant.

At Britt Moore Dental, we aim to be a true partner in your child’s dental care, starting them confidently on the road to a lifetime of great dental visits and lasting oral health. Contact our Houston office for personalized suggestions.
item.excerpt:encoded: A few simple preparations can make your child's dental visit a positive experience. Here are our best tips for parents.

item.title: Memorial Day and Getting Ready for Summer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Memorial Day has also evolved into a day that signifies the beginning of summer. As you prepare for the summer months, here are a few things to take care of to ensure your summertime is enjoyable:

  • Have your AC checked before the hottest days of summer.
  • Ensure home security while you’re away — use light timers and alert local police if you’ll be traveling.
  • Visit the doctor before vacation — including your dentist! It’s best to get any necessary dental work done before you travel.

Happy Memorial Day from everyone at Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: As Memorial Day signals the start of summer, here is a practical checklist to help your family prepare for a great season ahead.

item.title: Make Tooth Brushing Fun
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The recommended routine is two minutes of gentle brushing in the morning and two in the evening — but getting kids excited about brushing can be a challenge. Here are strategies to make dental hygiene more engaging:

  • Variety in Tools: Let children select their own colorful toothbrush and favorite toothpaste flavor to increase enthusiasm.
  • Incentive Systems: Simple rewards like sticker charts or choosing the bedtime story can encourage consistency.
  • Musical Timing: Use songs, stories, or dental apps to make the two-minute sessions feel shorter and more enjoyable.
  • Shared Experience: Brush together — it allows you to demonstrate proper technique while making it a bonding activity.

The habits your child develops now will form the foundation for a lifetime of oral health.
item.excerpt:encoded: Getting kids to brush their teeth can be a challenge. Here are practical tips to make tooth brushing fun and build lifelong habits.

item.title: Dental Tips for Your Summer Vacation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer is here, and it’s time to enjoy a well-deserved break! Take a few minutes to review these dental tips to keep your smile healthy all summer long.

Hydration: When you are active in warm weather, choose your drinks wisely. Sodas and sports drinks contain a lot of sugar, which encourages cavity-causing bacteria. Water is always a healthy, sugar-free choice — and if your tap water contains fluoride, you’re fighting cavities while staying hydrated.

Mouthguards: Biking, skateboarding, baseball, soccer — all great summer activities, but one fall or accidental contact can cause serious dental damage. If you don’t have a mouthguard, now is a great time to get one.

Vacation Plans: If your family is traveling, let us know! Get any necessary dental work done before you leave, and always carry our contact number in case a dental problem comes up while you’re away.

However you spend your summer, we hope it is filled with happy and healthy smiles!
item.excerpt:encoded: Keep your smile healthy all summer with tips on hydration, mouthguards, and dental care while traveling.

item.title: Celebrate Your Dad’s Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Father's Day is the perfect time to give Dad a gift that combines his interests with dental health benefits.

The Outdoor Dad: An emergency dental kit for camping adventures, containing cotton rolls, floss, pain relievers, and temporary filling material.

The High-Tech Dad: A smart electric toothbrush with multiple settings, pressure sensors, timing features, and app connectivity.

The Dad on the Go: A portable dental kit with travel toothbrush, toothpaste, mirror, picks, floss, mouthwash, and sugarless gum.

The Gourmet Dad: A curated gift basket featuring calcium-rich cheeses, fresh fruits, nuts, and dark chocolate (70% cacao or higher) for its antioxidant and cavity-fighting properties.

The Athletic Dad: A custom mouth guard offering superior comfort and protection compared to store-bought alternatives.

The Picture-Perfect Dad: Professional whitening treatments for a faster, longer-lasting bright smile. Happy Father's Day from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: This Father's Day, give Dad a gift that's both thoughtful and great for his oral health.

item.title: Fun Facts for the Fourth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The Fourth of July is a celebration bringing together friends and family for BBQs, games, fireworks, and festivities honoring American independence. Here are some fun facts to share this holiday:

The Statue of Liberty: The copper-plated lady weighs in at a whopping 450,000 tons and has been holding her torch up for more than 125 years. The broken shackles at her feet symbolize freedom from oppression.

Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest: Since 1916, this annual Coney Island competition on July 4th challenges participants to consume maximum hot dogs in ten minutes. The current record holder swallowed a whopping 68 hot dogs!

The History Behind Fireworks: Fireworks likely originated in China when alchemists stuffed early gunpowder into bamboo sticks to create powerful explosions, paving the way for today's modern fireworks.

Have a safe and happy Fourth of July from everyone at Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Celebrate Independence Day with these fun facts about the Statue of Liberty, fireworks, and famous Fourth of July traditions.

item.title: Satisfying a Sweet Tooth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Our biochemistry makes use of sugars on a cellular level — they are the body’s preferred energy source. But overconsumption of added sugars is linked to obesity, diabetes, heart disease, and premature aging. For dental health, bacteria in plaque feed on sugars and produce acids that attack tooth enamel and cause decay.

Be Choosy: Compare food labels for hidden sugars. Prioritize fruits over processed sweets, opt for dark chocolate, and select candies that won’t stick to teeth.

Watch Your Timing: Saliva can neutralize acids in the mouth, but it takes at least 20 minutes for the process to start. Enjoy desserts with meals rather than as stand-alone snacks.

Small Changes: Drink water after sweets, use straws with sugary beverages, and chew sugar-free gum to increase saliva production. Talk to our team about balanced approaches to sweets that protect your smile.
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn smart strategies for satisfying your sweet tooth while protecting your teeth from sugar-related damage.

item.title: Interproximal Cavities: The Inside Story
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Interproximal cavities develop on the touching surfaces between teeth, where toothbrush bristles cannot effectively reach. When plaque and food particles accumulate between teeth, bacteria produce acids that gradually erode tooth enamel.

Detection: These cavities may not be immediately obvious. Warning signs include chewing pain or sensitivity to hot, cold, or sweet foods. Early-stage cavities may present no symptoms at all, making regular exams and X-rays essential.

Treatment: Small cavities typically require fillings. Larger decay extending to the pulp may need root canal treatment followed by a crown. Severely decayed teeth might require extraction.

Prevention: Brush twice daily for two minutes and floss daily. Flossing really does the trick for interproximal cleaning. Ask our team about water flossers and interproximal brushes as additional tools.
item.excerpt:encoded: Interproximal cavities form between teeth where your brush can’t reach. Learn what they are, how to detect them, and how to prevent them.

item.title: Labor Day: Our Favorite Holiday to Rest!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Labor Day, celebrated on the first Monday each September, is a holiday devoted to honoring the American working community and their contributions to the country’s strength and prosperity.

The inaugural celebration occurred on September 5th, 1882, when the Central Labor Union held what they termed “a working man’s holiday.” By 1885, the observance had expanded throughout all American industries.
