We hope you celebrate safely and happily, whether locally in Houston, Texas or traveling elsewhere — and don’t forget to maintain your oral hygiene routine even on holiday! Happy Labor Day from Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: The history of Labor Day and how we celebrate the contributions of American workers. Happy Labor Day from Britt Moore Dental!

item.title: September is National Gum Care Month!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
September is National Gum Care Month, and it’s a great time to raise awareness about gum health and periodontal disease. Gingivitis — the early stage of gum disease — often goes undetected, making early recognition and action the most important steps to healthy gums.

Warning Signs of Gum Disease:

  • Red or swollen gums
  • Tender gums
  • Bleeding during brushing or flossing
  • Gum recession
  • Persistent bad breath
  • Loose teeth
  • Changes in bite alignment

Research consistently demonstrates connections between oral health and systemic health, particularly cardiovascular wellness. If you notice any of these symptoms, schedule an appointment promptly. Our Houston team can implement preventive measures and guide you on improving your daily oral hygiene.
item.excerpt:encoded: September is National Gum Care Month. Learn the warning signs of gum disease and why healthy gums are vital to your overall health.

item.title: What’s on Your Fall Reading List?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
How better to spend the fall months than inside by the fireplace with a warm cup of cider and a book in hand? Reading is vital to brain development at any age, and autumn is the perfect season to curl up with a great read.

Our team at Britt Moore Dental would love to know what you and your children are reading this fall. Share your recommendations with us — or if you’re looking for ideas, check with your local Houston librarian for seasonal suggestions.

Whether it’s fiction, nonfiction, children’s books, or classics, we’d love to hear your fall picks. Share them with us on our Facebook page or during your next appointment!
item.excerpt:encoded: Fall is the perfect season to curl up with a great book. Share your reading list with our team — we'd love to know what you're enjoying!

item.title: The Intriguing History of Halloween
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Halloween’s origins differ dramatically from modern celebrations. Originally called Samhain, the Celts observed this festival as their new year’s eve on November 1st, believing that the veil between this world and the spirit world is thinnest on this day, enabling communication with deceased relatives.

The holiday transformed when Christian missionaries incorporated Celtic practices into Christian observances. Pope Gregory the First instructed missionaries to work these traditions into the Christian calendar — leading to November 1st becoming All Saints Day.

Contemporary Halloween focuses on entertainment and sweets. Speaking of sweets — candy can cause major tooth decay and aggravate gum disease. We encourage families to offer some healthier alternatives like apples, and remind children to brush their teeth after consuming candy. Happy Halloween from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Did you know Halloween originated as a Celtic festival? Learn the fascinating history behind the holiday — and our dental tips for trick-or-treat season.

item.title: Getting Ready for Winter
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The winter season brings unique dental health challenges. Here are three key areas to focus on:

Stay Hydrated: Indoor heating, winter exercise, and reduced water intake during cold months can lead to dehydration and diminished saliva production. Saliva helps wash away food particles and bacteria, neutralizes cavity-causing acids, and strengthens enamel. Make a conscious effort to drink plenty of water.

Wear a Mouthguard for Winter Sports: Skiing, hockey, and basketball all pose dental injury risks. Options include over-the-counter pre-formed guards, boil-and-bite models, and custom-made guards from our office — custom versions offer superior comfort and fit.

Manage Winter Sensitivity: Cold-triggered tooth sensitivity may indicate cracked teeth, cavities, exposed dentin, receding gums, or aggressive brushing. Schedule a checkup with our Houston team to address any concerns.
item.excerpt:encoded: Winter brings special dental challenges — from dehydration to sports injuries and cold sensitivity. Here’s how to protect your smile this season.

item.title: My Teeth Don’t Line Up Anymore. Why?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
If your teeth no longer align properly, you may have temporomandibular joint disorder (TMD). This condition occurs when the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) — the hinge connecting your lower jaw to your skull — becomes inflamed or damaged.

The TMJ consists of two ball-and-socket joints, one on each side of your jaw. When inflamed or misaligned, these joints can cause significant discomfort and lead to tooth misalignment. Additionally, wisdom teeth can contribute to shifting; when wisdom teeth shift alongside TMD symptoms, extraction may be necessary.

Treatment Options: Your dentist may recommend dental orthotics, such as a lower jaw splint, to address the misalignment. If teeth don’t naturally return to proper positioning after wisdom tooth removal, dental splints may be used as a follow-up treatment.

If you experience tooth shifting, schedule an appointment with our Houston office to restore your smile.
item.excerpt:encoded: If your bite doesn't feel right anymore, you may be experiencing TMD. Learn about the causes and treatment options.

item.title: What’s the Advantage of Invisalign®?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When choosing between Invisalign and traditional braces, both options produce excellent results — but Invisalign offers significant advantages in comfort and convenience.

Comfort: Invisalign causes less pain, irritation, and soreness compared to braces since aligners are custom-fitted smooth plastic. There are also no dietary restrictions.

Brushing and Flossing: Invisalign aligners are easily removable, allowing patients to maintain normal oral hygiene routines without navigating wires and brackets.

Appearance: The aligners are practically invisible, enabling wearers to maintain normal social and professional lives without self-consciousness. This benefit applies equally to teenagers and adults.

Contact our Houston, Texas office for more information about Invisalign treatment options and whether they are right for you.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign offers real advantages over traditional braces — from comfort and appearance to easier brushing and no food restrictions.

item.title: Shark Teeth
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
If your child has a double row of teeth, you may be dealing with what dentists call “shark teeth” — when permanent adult teeth erupt behind still-rooted baby teeth. Normally, permanent teeth push against baby tooth roots, gradually dissolving them so the primary tooth loosens and falls out. Sometimes this dissolution fails, and the adult tooth must erupt elsewhere.

Shark teeth can first appear around ages five to seven when permanent front teeth start arriving, or several years later when adult molars come in. While it may look alarming, shark teeth generally aren’t serious. Treatment typically involves either waiting for the loose baby tooth to fall out naturally or having a dentist extract it — usually a straightforward procedure due to the smaller roots involved.

Once space becomes available, the permanent tooth will most often start moving to its proper position. Schedule a dental visit if baby teeth remain in place after several weeks, especially if your child is uncomfortable.
item.excerpt:encoded: Shark teeth — when adult teeth erupt behind baby teeth — can be alarming for parents. Here’s what causes them and when to see a dentist.

item.title: Team Dark Chocolate
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Valentine’s Day is a time to celebrate relationships with cards, gatherings, and treats. While stores fill with holiday sweets, our dental team recommends a healthier alternative: dark chocolate.

Health Benefits: Dark chocolate contains flavonoids — compounds found in cocoa beans that help protect the body against toxins, reduce blood pressure, and improve blood flow to the heart and brain.

Protective Properties for Teeth: According to Texas A&M Health Science Center, dark chocolate contains tannins that help prevent cavities by interfering with the bacteria that cause them. Unlike sticky candies, dark chocolate is less adhesive and won’t harbor cavity-causing bacteria as easily.

When selecting dark chocolate, opt for high-quality varieties with minimal processing and the highest cacao percentage you enjoy. Moderation is key. Happy Valentine’s Day from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: This Valentine's Day, our dental team is firmly on Team Dark Chocolate. Here's why it's better for your teeth than other sweets.

item.title: Start Your Day Off with a (Healthy) Smile!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Breakfast is the most important meal of the day — but some breakfast foods are better for your teeth than others. Cavity-causing bacteria use dietary sugars to produce acids that erode tooth enamel, so it pays to make informed choices.

Cereals: Check nutrition labels for added sugar content. Many whole grain cereals exist without excessive added sugars.

Juices: Choose 100% unsweetened fruit juice rather than “cocktails” or “punches” that contain significant added sugars.

Hidden Sugars: Low-fat yogurts often contain more added sugar than plain varieties. Smoothies can contain 70+ grams of sugar — homemade versions using fresh fruit are far preferable.

Occasional indulgences are fine. The goal is reducing added sugar by making informed substitutions — protecting both your overall health and your dental wellness.
item.excerpt:encoded: Your breakfast choices affect your dental health. Learn which common breakfast foods contain hidden sugars that can harm your teeth.

item.title: March is National Nutrition Month!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
March is the month the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics asks everyone to pay special attention to what goes into our bodies. And the foods you eat have a direct effect on the health of your teeth.

What Not to Eat: Bacteria rely on carbohydrates to thrive. That’s why we recommend cutting back on candy and sweets, which consist of simple sugars that feed bacteria and enhance tooth decay. Get in the habit of reading food labels and looking for products with added sugar, including ingredients ending with “-ose.”

What You Should Eat: Dairy products provide nutrients that support tooth enamel. Foods high in protein feature phosphorus, a nutrient critical to oral health. You can’t go wrong adding color to your plate with fruits and vegetables. Use some caution with highly acidic fruits like oranges — it’s better to include these foods in a full meal rather than eating them alone.

Good nutrition is something to prioritize all year long. March just serves as a helpful reminder that eating right is a proactive step in managing your dental health.
item.excerpt:encoded: March is National Nutrition Month. Learn which foods support your dental health and which ones to limit for a healthier smile.

item.title: St. Patrick’s Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
On March 17, everyone has a little Irish in them. St. Patrick’s Day is a joyous celebration of Irish heritage, originating as a commemoration of Saint Patrick, who brought Christianity to Ireland. St. Patrick’s Day has been an official public holiday in Ireland since 1903, celebrated with theater performances, music, fireworks, and festive parades.

Some fun facts: The Guinness World Records named St. Patrick’s Day the “Friendliest Day of the Year.” Montreal has held an annual St. Patrick’s Day parade since 1824. And the color originally associated with Saint Patrick was actually blue — it was his use of shamrocks to explain the Holy Trinity that made green the holiday’s signature color.

Don your greenest attire and have a wonderful St. Patrick’s Day from everyone at Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn the history behind St. Patrick's Day — including some surprising facts you probably didn't know. Happy St. Paddy's Day!

item.title: Keep It Cheesy for a Long-Lasting Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
You’ve likely heard the phrase “say cheese” countless times. But there’s a dental health reason to embrace cheese consumption beyond just smiling for cameras.

A study published in the May/June 2013 issue of General Dentistry discovered that consuming cheese increases plaque pH levels, creating a protective layer that may reduce cavity risk. The research involved 68 children ages 12–15 divided into three groups: those eating cheddar cheese, those drinking milk, and those consuming sugar-free yogurt. Only the cheese-eating group showed rapid pH increases in their mouths.

A separate study from the Journal of Contemporary Dental Practice found that cheese may also provide enamel protection against erosion from acidic beverages like soft drinks.

So embrace cheese as part of a tooth-friendly diet — it’s one of the more enjoyable ways to protect your smile!
item.excerpt:encoded: Research shows that eating cheese can raise oral pH levels and help protect your teeth from cavities. Here’s the science behind it.

item.title: Do Spring Allergies Mean (B)Looming Dental Problems?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Spring allergies don’t just make you sneeze — they can create unexpected dental complications as well. When pollen triggers the immune system to release histamines, tissues in the nose and sinuses become inflamed and mucus production increases.

Tooth Pain: The maxillary sinus cavities sit directly above upper molar roots. When sinus pressure builds, it can cause upper molar discomfort that mimics a toothache.

Dry Mouth: Mouth-breathing from nasal congestion reduces saliva flow, increasing cavity and infection risks while promoting gum irritation.

Postnasal Drip: Thickened mucus drainage causes hoarseness, coughing, sore throat, and difficulty swallowing.

Headaches & Facial Pain: Swollen tissue traps mucus inside sinus cavities, creating pressure that triggers discomfort throughout the face and head.

Consult your healthcare provider about antihistamines, maintain excellent oral hygiene, stay hydrated, and see our Houston team if dental symptoms persist throughout allergy season.
item.excerpt:encoded: Spring allergies can cause dental problems including tooth pain, dry mouth, and bad breath. Here’s how to protect your smile this season.

item.title: Caring for Your Invisalign® Aligners
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Caring for your Invisalign aligners is easy and uncomplicated. Follow these simple tips to keep your aligners in the best possible shape throughout your treatment.

Stay Clean:

  • Always brush and floss your teeth before using your aligners.
  • Brush your aligners with a separate soft toothbrush and lukewarm water.
  • Rinse your aligners whenever you remove them during the day.
  • Soak your aligners as recommended using the Invisalign Cleaning System or another dentist-approved solution.

Stay Clear:

  • White spots on aligners may mean plaque build-up — always rinse after removal.
  • Use only a soft brush and gentle pressure to avoid scratches.
  • Remove aligners when eating or drinking anything other than water — food and colored beverages cause staining.
  • Only soak aligners in appropriate solutions — harsh chemicals and colored mouthwashes can dim the clear plastic.

Talk to our team about the best products and methods for taking care of your aligners.
item.excerpt:encoded: Keeping your Invisalign aligners clean and clear is easier than you think. Follow these simple care tips for best results.

item.title: Earth Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Earth Day was conceived by Wisconsin Senator Gaylord Nelson, with the first celebration occurring on April 22, 1970, drawing a surprising 20 million participants. Today, Earth Day is celebrated in 175 countries where over 500 million people take part, making it the largest international environmental observation globally.

The Earth Day movement prompted Congress to establish the Environmental Protection Agency and pass the Endangered Species Act. Here are the Five R’s for sustainable living:

  • Reduce — Minimize unnecessary purchases and disposable items
  • Reuse — Use reusable bags, containers, and batteries
  • Recycle — Participate in municipal recycling programs
  • Re-buy — Purchase recycled or secondhand products
  • Rethink — Adopt eco-conscious practices like carpooling

The planet belongs to everyone, so everyone is equally responsible for protecting it. Happy Earth Day from Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Earth Day is celebrated by 500 million people in 175 countries. Here’s the history behind the holiday and five ways to celebrate year-round.

item.title: Summer is Almost Here: Tips for a Bright, White Smile!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer is almost here, which means a season full of vacations, adventures, and great memories is just around the corner. Everyone wants a glowing, radiant white smile when the sun comes around. Here are some reminders to keep your teeth healthy and beautiful:

Watch Your Beverages: Stay away from drinks that stain your teeth, like coffee, soft drinks, and dark-colored juices. These beverages weaken tooth enamel and darken your smile over time.

Maintain Your Oral Hygiene Routine: Focus on brushing regularly, even during busy summer schedules. After meals when full brushing isn’t possible, rinse your mouth with water to protect against staining.

Consider Professional Whitening: If you want a brighter smile in time for summer, ask our Houston team about professional whitening options that provide faster, longer-lasting results than over-the-counter alternatives.

Contact our office to schedule an appointment and get your smile summer-ready!
item.excerpt:encoded: Summer is just around the corner! Here are expert tips for keeping your teeth white, bright, and healthy all season long.

item.title: Memorial Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Memorial Day is not only a federal holiday in the United States, but a day of observance and remembrance of those who died in service. Originally known as Decoration Day, this solemn day has been marked since the end of the American Civil War as a day to commemorate soldiers who fought and died for our country.

By 1890, every state in the country was observing Decoration Day. The name formally changed to Memorial Day in 1967 to encompass all fallen American soldiers in all wars and conflicts. In June of 1968, Congress moved the official date to the last Monday in May to create a three-day weekend.

Traditional observances include raising the American Flag and then lowering it to half-staff until noon, then raising it again to full height — remembering those who gave their lives while affirming our willingness to honor their sacrifice.

From community parades and backyard cookouts to formal ceremonies, Memorial Day is commemorated in many ways. No matter how you choose to spend this day, take a moment to remember those who’ve lost their lives in an effort to preserve our freedom. Thank you to all who served — from everyone at Britt Moore Dental.
item.excerpt:encoded: Memorial Day is a day of remembrance for those who gave their lives in service to our country. Honoring their sacrifice from Britt Moore Dental.

item.title: Been a While? Come See Us!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
We understand that guilt is a powerful feeling. If it’s been a while since your last dental visit, know that our practice is a judgement-free zone. We’re just glad you’re here.

Here’s what to expect when you come back after a long absence:

Dental X-Rays: We’ll typically take X-rays, usually done every three to five years, to examine your dental structure and identify any potential issues.

Hygiene Appointment: This includes a review of your medical history followed by a thorough cleaning. Your hygienist will encourage you to share any oral health concerns and answer your questions.

Comprehensive Exam: The dentist will review findings from the hygiene appointment, discuss your medical history, address any remaining concerns, and outline treatment options for issues like cavities or broken fillings.

Just because life got in the way for a while doesn’t mean things have to stay that way! Call our Houston, Texas office to schedule your appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: If it's been a while since your last dental visit, there's no judgment here. Here's what to expect when you come back to see us.

item.title: Happy Fourth of July
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Every year, Americans celebrate the birth of the country and its independence on the Fourth of July through community parades, concerts, fireworks displays, and family gatherings. Here are some fun historical facts to share this holiday:

Although officially designated as a federal holiday in 1941, the tradition traces back to the American Revolution. The Declaration of Independence was adopted by delegates on July 4, 1776. Massachusetts became the first state to designate July 4th as Independence Day in 1781.

Fun Fourth of July facts: Stars on the original flag were arranged in a circle to symbolize colony equality. Americans consume over 150 million hot dogs on July 4th. Annual fireworks imports exceed $211 million. Benjamin Franklin preferred the turkey over the bald eagle as the national bird.

Have a safe and happy Fourth of July from everyone at Britt Moore Dental!
item.excerpt:encoded: Wishing everyone a safe and happy Fourth of July! Here are some fun historical facts about Independence Day from our team.

item.title: Steer Clear of That Candy!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
While candy may be tempting, consuming sugary treats in excess elevates the risk of tooth decay and can break down your teeth. Here are five recommendations for protecting your smile:

  1. Enjoy candy during meals when saliva can neutralize acids found in candies, particularly sour varieties.
  2. Avoid sticky or hard candies that linger in the mouth — prolonged acid exposure leads to cavities and decay.
  3. Drink fluoridated water, which research shows helps prevent cavities.
  4. Maintain daily oral hygiene by brushing twice daily and flossing at least once per day.
  5. Schedule dental checkups and cleanings twice yearly so we can identify cavities early and guide you on improving your oral health.

Contact our Houston, Texas office for more information on cavity prevention or to schedule your next appointment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Candy in excess leads to tooth decay. Here are five practical tips to protect your teeth when sweet cravings strike.

item.title: Teeth Whitening For a Bright Summer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Summer brings sunshine and warm weather, and many patients begin thinking about brightening their smiles. A whiter smile is available with just one visit at Britt Moore Dental.

Teeth whitening is safe, quick, and inexpensive, and can address tooth discolorations caused by staining, aging, or chemical damage. We utilize the latest whitening technology to help patients achieve the beautiful smile they desire.

Contact our Houston, Texas office to schedule an appointment and get your smile summer-ready!
item.excerpt:encoded: A brighter smile is just one visit away. Learn about professional teeth whitening and how it can refresh your look this summer.

item.title: Invisalign® and Invisalign Teen®
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Whether you’re an adult or a teenager who wants straighter teeth without traditional metal braces, Invisalign and Invisalign Teen offer an excellent solution.

Key Advantages: Dietary freedom - eat whatever you like without worrying about wires or brackets. Discretion - most people won’t notice you’re wearing them. Removability - aligners can be taken out when needed. Improved oral hygiene - standard brushing and flossing remain possible. Comfort - made from smooth BPA-free plastic. Teen-specific: up to six replacement aligners provided for lost or damaged sets.

After a consultation, we take X-rays, photos, and impressions to create 3D models showing your tooth movement throughout treatment. Patients receive new aligners every two weeks and must wear them 22 hours daily for best results. Contact our Houston office for more information.
item.excerpt:encoded: Invisalign and Invisalign Teen offer a discreet, comfortable way to straighten teeth for both adults and teenagers.

item.title: Caring for Your Smile after Invisalign® Treatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
After completing Invisalign treatment, maintaining your results requires ongoing commitment. Here are three essential practices for post-treatment care.

Retainers are Critical: Following orthodontic work, patients typically need retainers to prevent teeth from shifting back. Wear them as directed - not wearing retainers could result in shifting teeth and potentially undo your results.

Daily Oral Hygiene Matters: Continue daily flossing to prevent plaque buildup. Your teeth may experience temporary sensitivity after treatment - sensitive toothpaste can help, and a warm saltwater rinse may relieve discomfort during the first week or two.

Regular Professional Checkups: Scheduling routine dental exams ensures long-term dental health through professional cleanings, X-rays, and any needed cavity treatment.
item.excerpt:encoded: Your Invisalign treatment is complete - now what? Here’s how to care for your smile and protect your results long-term.

item.title: Why Do Wisdom Teeth Need to Be Removed?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Wisdom teeth are the final molars to emerge in adults, also called third molars. Not everyone requires wisdom tooth extraction, but specific situations warrant removal when teeth are impacted or causing problems.

Impaction occurs when wisdom teeth cannot properly emerge through the gums. Types include soft tissue impaction (gum tissue covers the tooth), partial bony impaction (gum and jawbone partially cover the tooth), and complete bony impaction (fully covered by gum and bone).

Impacted wisdom teeth are typically removed to prevent serious infections, including pericoronitis - an acute abscess that develops when food and bacteria lodge beneath the gum flap, causing pain, swelling, and abscess formation.

Regular dental checkups allow us to monitor wisdom teeth development and identify potential problems before they become severe. Contact our Houston office to schedule your evaluation.
item.excerpt:encoded: Not everyone needs their wisdom teeth removed - but impaction and infection risk often make extraction necessary. Here’s what you need to know.
